118	@dataclass(frozen=True)
119	class AssetSummary:
120	    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset.
121	
122	    **Two orthogonal health axes (#803).** The old single "health" conflated them:
123	
124	    - *Suite health* (data quality, ADR 0005) — ``worst_severity`` over the
125	      **evaluated** checks, plus ``checks_total``/``checks_passed``. Operational
126	      results never rank here, so a datasource DataQ couldn't reach reads as "no
127	      data", never as a green "passing" nor as a red data failure.
128	    - *Connection health* (reachability) — ``has_operational_error`` /
129	      ``has_skip``: could DataQ execute against the datasource at all? Derived
130	      **purely from the runs already recorded** (a latest run that `failed`, or
131	      any ``error``/``skip`` result on one) — there is no connection-probe polling
132	      loop behind this, by design.
133	    """
134	
135	    id: uuid.UUID
136	    namespace: str
137	    # ``None`` = a REDACTED row (#920): the asset exists, is monitored by suites the
138	    # caller holds no grant on, and browse includes it as an anonymous entry rather
139	    # than omitting it — the tree-level twin of the lineage graph's #845 rule
140	    # (omission asserts "this schema holds nothing else"). The namespace stays (the
141	    # tree needs the placement — a deliberate, user-directed disclosure); the NAME
142	    # is the protected fact, along with env/description/owner and every health
143	    # field (forced to their empty defaults — a hidden asset's health is itself a
144	    # fact about it). The detail endpoint keeps 404ing these no-leak.
145	    name: str | None
146	    env: str | None
147	    description: str | None
148	    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
149	    # None on redacted rows (#920) — liveness cadence is a fact about the asset.
150	    last_seen: datetime | None
151	    suite_count: int
152	    # ── suite health (data quality) ──
153	    worst_severity: str | None
154	    checks_total: int
155	    checks_passed: int
156	    last_run_at: datetime | None
157	    # ── connection health (reachability / execution) ──
158	    # `has_failed_run`: any latest run whose *execution* `failed` (wrote no results).
159	    # `has_active_run`: any latest run still `queued`/`running` (hasn't concluded).
160	    # `has_cancelled_run`: any latest run `cancelled`. A cancelled run proves
161	    #   nothing — if it was killed before a single check ran, we may never have
162	    #   reached the datasource at all, so it must not roll up green.
163	    # `has_operational_error`: a failed run OR any `error` result — DataQ could not
164	    #   evaluate against the datasource. `has_skip`: any `skip` result (a
165	    #   precondition, e.g. the batch hasn't landed, wasn't met) — degraded, not down.
166	    has_failed_run: bool = False
167	    has_active_run: bool = False
168	    has_cancelled_run: bool = False
169	    has_operational_error: bool = False
170	    has_skip: bool = False
171	    is_accessible: bool = True
172	    # Redacted rows only (#920): the non-leaf path segments (db/schema or folder),
173	    # pre-split server-side so client and server can never disagree on the
174	    # separator rule. None on full rows and single-segment redacted names.
175	    name_prefix_segments: list[str] | None = None
176

178	@dataclass(frozen=True)
179	class LineageNode:
180	    """A lineage neighbour — enough to render, no run data (ADR 0034 §2).
181	
182	    ``depth`` is the hop distance from the asset under view (1 = a direct
183	    neighbour), which is what lets the UI lay the graph out in columns (#805)
184	    instead of flattening every hop into one list.
185	
186	    **A neighbour outside the caller's grants is REDACTED, not omitted (#845).** The
187	    lineage walk is not authz-scoped — it can't be, because the graph's job is blast
188	    radius, and a table's real consumers do not stop existing because you can't see
189	    them. But the asset endpoint 404s those assets *no-leak* (ADR 0034 decision 5), so
190	    handing their ``name``/``namespace``/``env`` to a non-grantee through the graph
191	    would defeat that guarantee one click earlier — and did.
192	
193	    So an inaccessible neighbour keeps only ``id`` (an opaque UUID; the edges need it
194	    to draw the shape) and ``depth``: identity fields are ``None`` and ``is_monitored``
195	    is forced ``False``. The user still learns that *something* is downstream — which
196	    keeps the blast radius honest instead of asserting the confident falsehood
197	    "nothing consumes this table" (the #828/#823 lesson: never fix a leak by shipping a
198	    lie). What they don't learn is *what* it is.
199	
200	    Redaction is done **here, server-side**: a name that is hidden in CSS has still
201	    crossed the wire.
202	    """
203	
204	    id: uuid.UUID
205	    namespace: str | None
206	    name: str | None
207	    env: str | None
208	    is_monitored: bool
209	    depth: int = 1
210	    is_accessible: bool = True
211

214	class LineageEdgeRef:
215	    """One edge of the neighbourhood DAG, as ``(upstream → downstream)`` asset ids.
216	
217	    The UI draws exactly these; without them a graph could only *guess* which node
218	    at depth 2 hangs off which node at depth 1 (#805).
219	
220	    ``columns`` is the edge's column-level refinement (#901) when a warehouse source
221	    recorded one — ``[upstream_column, downstream_column]`` pairs, unioned across the
222	    sources that observed the edge. **Redacted server-side by the #845 one-rule**: when
223	    either endpoint is outside the caller's grants, the pairs are withheld and only
224	    ``column_count`` survives — a column name is schema disclosure, the exact class the
225	    node redaction exists to prevent, and a name hidden in CSS has still crossed the
226	    wire. ``column_count`` is present whenever the edge has column data (it is the
227	    redacted box's honest label: *something* maps, in N column links).
228	    """
229	
230	    source: uuid.UUID
231	    target: uuid.UUID
232	    columns: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] | None = None
233	    column_count: int | None = None

299	def _latest_run_per_suite(session: Session, suite_ids: list[uuid.UUID]) -> dict[uuid.UUID, Run]:
300	    """The most-recent run for each suite (DISTINCT ON, newest `created_at`)."""
301	    if not suite_ids:
302	        return {}
303	    rows = session.scalars(
304	        select(Run)
305	        .where(Run.suite_id.in_(suite_ids))
306	        .distinct(Run.suite_id)
307	        .order_by(Run.suite_id, Run.created_at.desc())
308	    )
309	    return {run.suite_id: run for run in rows}
310	
311	
312	def _run_outcome(
313	    run: Run | None,
314	    outcome: tuple[int, int, str | None] | None,
315	    op_flags: tuple[bool, bool] | None = None,
316	) -> RunOutcome:
317	    """Assemble a `RunOutcome` from a suite's latest run + its check-outcome tuple
318	    + its operational (`error`/`skip`) flags."""
319	    if run is None:
320	        return RunOutcome()
321	    total, passed, worst = outcome or (0, 0, None)
322	    has_error, has_skip = op_flags or (False, False)
323	    return RunOutcome(
324	        run_id=run.id,
325	        status=run.status,
326	        worst_severity=worst,
327	        checks_total=total,
328	        checks_passed=passed,
329	        finished_at=run.finished_at,
330	        created_at=run.created_at,
331	        has_error=has_error,
332	        has_skip=has_skip,
333	    )
334	
335	
336	def _composing_suites(
337	    suites: list[Suite],
338	    levels: dict[uuid.UUID, str | None],
339	    latest_runs: dict[uuid.UUID, Run],
340	    outcomes: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]],
341	    op_flags: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[bool, bool]] | None = None,
342	) -> list[ComposingSuite]:
343	    """Build the per-suite breakdown for one asset's suites (sorted by name)."""
344	    op_flags = op_flags or {}
345	    composing: list[ComposingSuite] = []
346	    for suite in suites:
347	        level = levels.get(suite.id)
348	        if level is None:  # defensive: only reachable suites are passed in
349	            continue
350	        run = latest_runs.get(suite.id)
351	        outcome = outcomes.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
352	        flags = op_flags.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
353	        composing.append(
354	            ComposingSuite(
355	                suite_id=suite.id,
356	                name=suite.name,
357	                my_permission=level,
358	                latest_run=_run_outcome(run, outcome, flags),
359	            )
360	        )
361	    return composing
362	
363	
364	def _roll_up(asset: Asset, composing: list[ComposingSuite]) -> AssetSummary:
365	    """Roll a set of composing suites up into the asset-level health summary."""
366	    statuses: list[str] = []
367	    checks_total = checks_passed = 0
368	    last_run_at: datetime | None = None
369	    has_failed_run = has_active_run = has_cancelled_run = False
370	    has_operational_error = has_skip = False
371	    for suite in composing:
372	        run = suite.latest_run
373	        if run.worst_severity is not None:
374	            statuses.append(run.worst_severity)
375	        # Execution state, distinct from check severity (see AssetSummary): a
376	        # `failed` run wrote no results and must not roll up green; an active run
377	        # hasn't concluded yet.
378	        if run.status == "failed":
379	            has_failed_run = True
380	        elif run.status in ("queued", "running"):
381	            has_active_run = True
382	        elif run.status == "cancelled":
383	            has_cancelled_run = True
384	        # Connection health (#803): a run that failed outright, or one that ran but
385	        # whose checks threw, both mean DataQ could not evaluate against the
386	        # datasource. `skip` is weaker — it executed, a precondition just wasn't met.
387	        if run.status == "failed" or run.has_error:
388	            has_operational_error = True
389	        if run.has_skip:
390	            has_skip = True
391	        checks_total += run.checks_total
392	        checks_passed += run.checks_passed
393	        ts = run.finished_at or run.created_at
394	        if ts is not None and (last_run_at is None or ts > last_run_at):
395	            last_run_at = ts
396	    return AssetSummary(
397	        id=asset.id,
398	        namespace=asset.namespace,
399	        name=asset.name,
400	        env=asset.env,
401	        description=asset.description,
402	        owner_user_id=asset.owner_user_id,
403	        last_seen=asset.last_seen,
404	        suite_count=len(composing),
405	        worst_severity=worst_severity(statuses),
406	        checks_total=checks_total,
407	        checks_passed=checks_passed,
408	        last_run_at=last_run_at,
409	        has_failed_run=has_failed_run,
410	        has_active_run=has_active_run,
411	        has_cancelled_run=has_cancelled_run,
412	        has_operational_error=has_operational_error,
413	        has_skip=has_skip,
414	    )

417	# ── public API ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
418	
419	
420	def list_visible_assets(
421	    session: Session,
422	    *,
423	    user_id: uuid.UUID,
424	    include_all: bool = False,
425	    limit: int = 200,
426	    offset: int = 0,
427	) -> list[AssetSummary]:
428	    """Assets the caller can see (via ≥1 accessible composing suite), sorted by
429	    ``(namespace, name)`` and paginated with ``limit``/``offset``.
430	
431	    Pagination is applied at the SQL level over the *asset* page (not the suite
432	    rows), so a page is a stable, deterministic slice regardless of how many
433	    suites compose each asset. Aggregation is filtered to the caller's grants: a
434	    partial-grant caller sees the asset but only the suites/runs they can view
435	    roll up into its health."""
436	    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
437	    # Browse shows what actually exists — ALL of it (#846, extended by #920):
438	    #
439	    # - assets whose suites you can view → full rows;
440	    # - suite-less assets → full rows (#846 — redaction protects a grant, and an
441	    #   asset nobody granted is protected by nothing);
442	    # - assets monitored ONLY by suites you can't see → **REDACTED rows** (#920,
443	    #   user-directed): omitting them asserted "this schema holds nothing else" —
444	    #   the same falsehood the lineage graph's #845 rule exists to prevent, one
445	    #   surface over. The row keeps id + placement and nothing else; the detail
446	    #   endpoint keeps 404ing it.
447	    #
448	    # Accessibility is derived by `_accessible_asset_ids` — the SAME helper the
449	    # lineage graph redacts with (#911-review: a second SQL encoding of the rule
450	    # here would be the #845/#847 drift class reborn). Ordering note: redacted rows
451	    # sort by their true (hidden) name — a weak alphabetical-position signal,
452	    # accepted with the placement disclosure.
453	    assets = list(
454	        session.scalars(
455	            select(Asset).order_by(Asset.namespace, Asset.name).limit(limit).offset(offset)
456	        )
457	    )
458	    if not assets:
459	        return []
460	    page_ids = [a.id for a in assets]
461	    open_ids = _accessible_asset_ids(
462	        session,
463	        page_ids,
464	        user_id=user_id,
465	        include_all=include_all,
466	        has_suite=_monitored_ids(session, page_ids),
467	    )
468	
469	    suites = list(
470	        session.scalars(
471	            select(Suite)
472	            .where(Suite.asset_id.in_(list(open_ids)), Suite.id.in_(accessible))
473	            .order_by(Suite.name)
474	        )
475	    )
476	    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
477	    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
478	    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
479	    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
480	    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
481	
482	    suites_by_asset: dict[uuid.UUID, list[Suite]] = defaultdict(list)
483	    for suite in suites:
484	        assert suite.asset_id is not None  # filtered in the query
485	        suites_by_asset[suite.asset_id].append(suite)
486	
487	    return [
488	        (
489	            _roll_up(
490	                asset,
491	                _composing_suites(
492	                    suites_by_asset.get(asset.id, []), levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags
493	                ),
494	            )
495	            if asset.id in open_ids
496	            else _redacted_summary(asset)
497	        )
498	        for asset in assets
499	    ]
500	
501	
502	def _redacted_summary(asset: Asset) -> AssetSummary:
503	    """A #920 redacted browse row: id + namespace + the PARENT path only — the leaf
504	    name is the protected fact, along with env/description/owner and every health
505	    axis (empty defaults — a hidden asset's health, monitoredness, and suite count
506	    are facts about it).
507	
508	    ``name_prefix_segments`` (db/schema or folder path, leaf stripped, ALREADY
509	    segmented server-side with the same separator rule the tree uses) is a
510	    deliberate, user-directed disclosure: the tree places the 🔒 row inside its
511	    real group (`DATAQ_DB → ANALYTICS → Restricted`) — the same placement the
512	    lineage graph already reveals for any redacted node connected by an edge.
513	    Shipping segments, not a joined string, keeps one segmentation rule: a
514	    re-split client-side would re-detect the separator on the PREFIX (which can
515	    differ from the full name's — e.g. a dotted directory under a slashed path)
516	    and file the locked row in a fabricated group."""
517	    sep = "/" if "/" in asset.name else "."
518	    segments = [part for part in asset.name.split(sep) if part]
519	    return AssetSummary(
520	        id=asset.id,
521	        namespace=asset.namespace,
522	        name=None,
523	        name_prefix_segments=segments[:-1] or None,
524	        env=None,
525	        description=None,
526	        owner_user_id=None,
527	        # Liveness is a fact about the hidden asset too (watching it tick reveals
528	        # the pipeline's cadence) — withheld like everything else (#921 review).
529	        last_seen=None,
530	        suite_count=0,
531	        worst_severity=None,
532	        checks_total=0,
533	        checks_passed=0,
534	        last_run_at=None,
535	        is_accessible=False,
536	    )
537	
538	
539	def get_visible_asset(
540	    session: Session, asset_id: uuid.UUID, *, user_id: uuid.UUID, include_all: bool = False
541	) -> AssetDetail:
542	    """One asset's detail (aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage).
543	
544	    Raises `AssetNotFoundError` (404) if the asset does not exist or the caller can
545	    view no suite targeting it — the two are indistinguishable (no-leak). A
546	    workspace-admin (``include_all``) sees every asset, **including suite-less
547	    orphans** (e.g. a lineage-only node, or an asset whose last composing suite was
548	    deleted) — for them only a truly unknown id 404s."""
549	    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
550	    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
551	    suites = list(
552	        session.scalars(
553	            select(Suite)
554	            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset_id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
555	            .order_by(Suite.name)
556	        )
557	    )
558	    # No-leak: an asset whose suites the caller can ALL not see is indistinguishable from
559	    # one that doesn't exist. That is the grant boundary, and it stays closed.
560	    #
561	    # A **suite-less** asset is not behind that boundary (ADR 0034 amendment, #845/#846):
562	    # no suites means no grant to withhold and nothing behind it — no runs, no results, no
563	    # samples. It opens to an honest, empty page (identity + lineage, no health). Hiding it
564	    # only produced the dead link that surfaced #845: the lineage graph must draw it (else
565	    # the graph claims the table feeds nothing), and a node the graph draws must be a node
566	    # the endpoint opens.
567	    if asset is None:
568	        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
569	    if not suites and not include_all and _monitored_ids(session, [asset_id]):
570	        # It HAS suites — the caller simply can't view any of them. 404, no-leak.
571	        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
572	
573	    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
574	    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
575	    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
576	    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
577	    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
578	    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)
579	
580	    summary = _roll_up(asset, composing)
581	    graph = lineage_neighbourhood(session, asset_id)
582	    neighbour_ids = [a.id for a, _ in graph.upstream] + [a.id for a, _ in graph.downstream]
583	    # ONE lookup of "which of these assets has any suite" — it answers both questions
584	    # below (is it monitored? could a grant even exist for it?), which are the same fact.
585	    has_suite = _monitored_ids(session, neighbour_ids)
586	    # The lineage walk is not authz-scoped (blast radius must stay true), so the caller's
587	    # own visibility is applied HERE, at the boundary — a neighbour they hold no grant for
588	    # is redacted to an anonymous node rather than handed over (#845).
589	    accessible_assets = _accessible_asset_ids(
590	        session,
591	        neighbour_ids,
592	        user_id=user_id,
593	        include_all=include_all,
594	        has_suite=has_suite,
595	    )
596	    return AssetDetail(
597	        summary=summary,
598	        suites=composing,
599	        upstream=_lineage_nodes(graph.upstream, has_suite, accessible_assets),
600	        downstream=_lineage_nodes(graph.downstream, has_suite, accessible_assets),
601	        # The viewed asset is accessible by definition (this endpoint already authz'd
602	        # it); neighbours use the same accessibility set the node redaction derives
603	        # from, so the edge-level and node-level redaction can never disagree (#845).
604	        lineage_edges=_lineage_edge_refs(
605	            session, graph.edges, accessible={asset_id} | accessible_assets
606	        ),
607	        # Lineage-source health names ORCHESTRATION CONNECTIONS (name, type, classified
608	        # poll error) — infrastructure information, not asset information. It is shown to
609	        # a caller with a stake in this asset (≥1 suite on it) or an admin; a caller who
610	        # merely reached a suite-less asset through browse has no stake and is not handed
611	        # the workspace's connection inventory. Without this gate, the #846 visibility
612	        # widening would have quietly widened an infra disclosure too (#848 review).
613	        failing_lineage_sources=(
614	            failing_lineage_sources(session) if (suites or include_all) else []
615	        ),
616	        # Same stake gate as failing_lineage_sources — it names workspace connections
617	        # (infra), so only a caller with a suite on this asset (or an admin) sees it.
618	        warehouse_lineage_status=(
619	            warehouse_lineage_status(session) if (suites or include_all) else []
620	        ),
621	    )
622	
623	
624	def warehouse_lineage_status(session: Session) -> list[WarehouseLineageStatus]:
625	    """Warehouse-native lineage sources that are degraded or failing (#858 slice 4).
626	
627	    Workspace-wide (like `failing_lineage_sources`): a warehouse's lineage tier is a
628	    property of the source, not the asset. Lists only Snowflake / Unity Catalog
629	    connections that have refreshed at least once AND are either degraded (a coarser
630	    tier — e.g. Snowflake OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES because the account isn't Enterprise) or
631	    errored (the last refresh failed). A healthy full-tier source is omitted — no banner
632	    over a clean, current graph.
633	    """
634	    rows = session.scalars(
635	        select(Connection).where(
636	            Connection.type.in_(("snowflake", "unity_catalog")),
637	            Connection.lineage_last_refresh_at.is_not(None),
638	            or_(
639	                Connection.lineage_degraded_reason.is_not(None),
640	                Connection.lineage_last_error.is_not(None),
641	            ),
642	        )
643	    ).all()
644	    return [
645	        WarehouseLineageStatus(
646	            connection_id=c.id,
647	            name=c.name,
648	            type=c.type,
649	            tier=c.lineage_last_tier,
650	            degraded_reason=c.lineage_degraded_reason,
651	            last_error=c.lineage_last_error,
652	            last_refreshed_at=c.lineage_last_refresh_at,
653	        )
654	        for c in rows
655	    ]
656	
657	
658	def failing_lineage_sources(session: Session) -> list[LineageSourceHealth]:
659	    """Lineage-feeding connections whose poll is currently failing (#828).
660	
661	    Workspace-wide, not per-asset, and deliberately so: lineage arrives from a *source*
662	    (a dbt project's manifest), not from the asset. If that source is down, every asset's
663	    lineage is suspect — including the ones that legitimately have none — so the caveat
664	    belongs on all of them.
665	
666	    Scoped to `dbt` because it is the only orchestration provider that feeds lineage
667	    today (`read_manifest`, #759). Widening it means adding a provider, not rewriting
668	    this: the filter rides the existing capability, not a hardcoded list of names.
669	    """
670	    rows = session.scalars(
671	        select(Connection).where(
672	            Connection.type == "dbt",
673	            Connection.consecutive_poll_failures > 0,
674	        )
675	    ).all()
676	    return [
677	        LineageSourceHealth(
678	            connection_id=c.id,
679	            name=c.name,
680	            type=c.type,
681	            consecutive_failures=c.consecutive_poll_failures,
682	            last_error=c.last_poll_error,
683	            last_polled_at=c.last_polled_at,
684	        )
685	        for c in rows
686	    ]
687	
688	
689	def summarize_asset(
690	    session: Session, asset: Asset, *, user_id: uuid.UUID, include_all: bool = False
691	) -> AssetSummary:
692	    """Roll one already-loaded asset up into its list-row summary.
693	
694	    Unlike `get_visible_asset`, this never 404s on "no composing suites" — an asset
695	    with zero suites rolls up to an empty (no-run) health summary. Used by the
696	    admin PATCH response, where the asset need not have suites to have metadata."""
697	    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
698	    suites = list(
699	        session.scalars(
700	            select(Suite)
701	            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset.id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
702	            .order_by(Suite.name)
703	        )
704	    )
705	    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id) if suites else {}
706	    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
707	    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
708	    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
709	    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
710	    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)
711	    return _roll_up(asset, composing)
712	
713	
714	def _monitored_ids(session: Session, ids: list[uuid.UUID]) -> set[uuid.UUID]:
715	    """Which of ``ids`` have ≥1 suite targeting them (globally — a structural fact,
716	    not a grant). One grouped query for the whole neighbourhood (no N+1)."""
717	    if not ids:
718	        return set()
719	    return {
720	        asset_id
721	        for (asset_id,) in session.execute(
722	            select(Suite.asset_id).where(Suite.asset_id.in_(ids)).group_by(Suite.asset_id)
723	        )
724	    }
725	
726	
727	def _accessible_asset_ids(
728	    session: Session,
729	    ids: list[uuid.UUID],
730	    *,
731	    user_id: uuid.UUID,
732	    include_all: bool,
733	    has_suite: set[uuid.UUID],
734	) -> set[uuid.UUID]:
735	    """Of ``ids``, the assets the caller may see — the one visibility rule (#845).
736	
737	    An asset is visible when **either**:
738	
739	    - the caller can view ≥1 suite targeting it (ADR 0034 decision 5 — authz derived from
740	      the suite ladder, never granted separately); **or**
741	    - it has **no suites at all** (ADR 0034 amendment, #845/#846).
742	
743	    The second clause is the point, and it is not a loophole: redaction exists to protect
744	    a *grant*, and an asset nobody has granted is protected by nothing. A suite-less asset
745	    (a raw source table, a dbt mart nobody monitors yet, an asset whose last suite was
746	    deleted — its runs/results cascade with it, #540) has no runs, no results and no
747	    samples behind it. The only thing withheld was its **name** — which the lineage graph
748	    reveals the existence of anyway.
749	
750	    Keeping those hidden forced a worse lie than the one #845 fixes: every raw upstream
751	    would render "🔒 Restricted" to a non-admin, when it isn't restricted at all — it's
752	    merely unmonitored. Lineage would be a wall of locked boxes.
753	
754	    So the only thing we withhold is an asset that someone *else* monitors and you may
755	    not — exactly the grant boundary the no-leak 404 defends.
756	
757	    A workspace-admin (``include_all``) sees everything (ADR 0027).
758	
759	    ``has_suite`` is the caller-supplied "assets that ANY suite targets" set — the only
760	    assets a grant could exist for. It is passed in rather than re-queried because the
761	    caller has already computed it (it is the same set the ``is_monitored`` flag reads),
762	    and querying it twice made the two derivations independent when they are in fact the
763	    same fact about the same rows.
764	    """
765	    if not ids:
766	        return set()
767	    if include_all:
768	        return set(ids)
769	    granted = {
770	        asset_id
771	        for (asset_id,) in session.execute(
772	            select(Suite.asset_id)
773	            .where(Suite.asset_id.in_(ids), Suite.id.in_(accessible_suite_ids(user_id)))
774	            .group_by(Suite.asset_id)
775	        )
776	    }
777	    # The granted ones, plus the suite-less ones: an id absent from `has_suite` is
778	    # targeted by no suite at all, so nothing is being kept from anyone.
779	    return granted | (set(ids) - has_suite)
780	
781	
782	def _lineage_edge_refs(
783	    session: Session,
784	    edges: list[tuple[uuid.UUID, uuid.UUID]],
785	    *,
786	    accessible: set[uuid.UUID],
787	) -> list[LineageEdgeRef]:
788	    """The neighbourhood's edges with their column-level refinement (#901), redacted
789	    by the #845 one-rule: an edge touching a redacted node yields ``column_count``
790	    only — the pairs (column NAMES of an asset the caller can't see) never leave the
791	    server. Column data is unioned across the sources that observed the edge (two
792	    provenance rows for one asset pair are one drawn edge)."""
793	    if not edges:
794	        return []
795	    pairs: dict[tuple[uuid.UUID, uuid.UUID], set[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
796	    for up, down, cols in session.execute(
797	        select(
798	            LineageEdge.upstream_asset_id,
799	            LineageEdge.downstream_asset_id,
800	            LineageEdge.columns,
801	        ).where(
802	            tuple_(LineageEdge.upstream_asset_id, LineageEdge.downstream_asset_id).in_(edges),
803	            LineageEdge.columns.is_not(None),
804	            # Exclude JSON 'null' in SQL (#907) — rows bulk-written before
805	            # `none_as_null` carry it and pass `is_not(None)`.
806	            func.jsonb_typeof(LineageEdge.columns) != "null",
807	        )
808	    ):
809	        # Defensive shape check: `columns` is app-written JSONB, but a malformed
810	        # value must degrade to "skipped", never 500 the asset page — LOUDLY (#907
811	        # review: a silent skip is the confident-empty-state failure mode #828
812	        # taught; an operator must be able to see the backfill missed rows).
813	        if not isinstance(cols, (list, tuple)):
814	            log.warning(
815	                "lineage_edge_columns_malformed",
816	                upstream_asset_id=str(up),
817	                downstream_asset_id=str(down),
818	                value_type=type(cols).__name__,
819	            )
820	            continue
821	        bucket = pairs.setdefault((up, down), set())
822	        bucket.update(
823	            (str(entry[0]), str(entry[1]))
824	            for entry in cols
825	            if isinstance(entry, (list, tuple)) and len(entry) == 2
826	        )
827	    refs: list[LineageEdgeRef] = []
828	    for up, down in edges:
829	        cols_set = pairs.get((up, down))
830	        if not cols_set:
831	            refs.append(LineageEdgeRef(source=up, target=down))
832	            continue
833	        both_visible = up in accessible and down in accessible
834	        refs.append(
835	            LineageEdgeRef(
836	                source=up,
837	                target=down,
838	                columns=tuple(sorted(cols_set)) if both_visible else None,
839	                column_count=len(cols_set),
840	            )
841	        )
842	    return refs
843	
844	
845	def _lineage_nodes(
846	    assets: list[tuple[Asset, int]],
847	    monitored: set[uuid.UUID],
848	    accessible: set[uuid.UUID],
849	) -> list[LineageNode]:
850	    """Map reachable lineage assets (+ their hop depth) to render-only nodes,
851	    **redacting the ones outside the caller's grants** (#845 — see `LineageNode`).
852	
853	    A redacted node keeps its id (the edges reference it) and its depth, and nothing
854	    else: no name, no namespace, no env, and ``is_monitored`` forced ``False`` rather
855	    than reported — whether someone else monitors an asset you can't see is itself a
856	    fact about that asset.
857	    """
858	    return [
859	        LineageNode(
860	            id=a.id,
861	            namespace=a.namespace if a.id in accessible else None,
862	            name=a.name if a.id in accessible else None,
863	            env=a.env if a.id in accessible else None,
864	            is_monitored=a.id in monitored and a.id in accessible,
865	            depth=depth,
866	            is_accessible=a.id in accessible,
867	        )
868	        for a, depth in assets
869	    ]
870	
871	
872	def update_asset_metadata(
873	    session: Session,
874	    asset_id: uuid.UUID,
875	    *,
876	    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
877	    description: str | None = None,
878	    set_owner: bool = False,
879	    set_description: bool = False,
880	) -> Asset:
881	    """Set an asset's owner and/or description (workspace-Admin-only; gated at API).
882	
883	    ``set_owner`` / ``set_description`` are the partial-update discriminators, so a
884	    field can be explicitly cleared to ``None`` versus left untouched (mirrors the
885	    suite PATCH's None-means-leave-alone problem, made explicit). Raises
886	    `AssetNotFoundError` (404) for an unknown id — no authz derivation here, since a
887	    workspace-admin sees every asset (ADR 0027) — and `AssetOwnerInvalidError` (422)
888	    when ``owner_user_id`` names no existing user (FK pre-check, never a raw 500)."""
889	    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
890	    if asset is None:
891	        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
892	    if set_owner:
893	        if owner_user_id is not None and session.get(User, owner_user_id) is None:
894	            raise AssetOwnerInvalidError(
895	                "owner user does not exist", detail={"owner_user_id": str(owner_user_id)}
896	            )
897	        asset.owner_user_id = owner_user_id
898	    if set_description:
899	        asset.description = description
900	    session.commit()
901	    session.refresh(asset)
902	    log.info("asset_metadata_updated", asset_id=str(asset.id))
903	    return asset
904

1	"""Read-only asset view API (ADR 0034, gap G-d phase 2, #760).
2	
3	Assets are the browse/reason grain over the suite execution grain. This surface
4	lists the assets a caller can see and drills into one — its composing suites +
5	their latest run health + the lineage neighbourhood.
6	
7	**Authz is derived, never granted (ADR 0027 / ADR 0034 decision 5):** an asset is
8	visible iff the caller can `view` ≥1 suite targeting it; the aggregation is
9	filtered to the caller's grants; a workspace-admin sees all; an asset wholly
10	outside the caller's grants is 404-no-leak. All of that lives in
11	`asset_view_service` (which reuses the suites/runs visibility subquery), so this
12	module is a thin HTTP layer.
13	
14	Asset-metadata mutation (`PATCH`) is **workspace-Admin-only** (ADR 0034 §4) —
15	gated by `require_workspace_admin`, the same 403 the /admin surface uses.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import uuid
21	from datetime import datetime
22	from typing import Annotated
23	
24	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Query
25	from pydantic import ConfigDict, Field
26	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
27	
28	from backend.app.api.v1._base import ApiModel
29	from backend.app.core.auth import get_current_user, is_workspace_admin, require_workspace_admin
30	from backend.app.db.models import User
31	from backend.app.db.session import get_db
32	from backend.app.services import asset_view_service as svc
33	
34	router = APIRouter(tags=["assets"])
35

52	    """One suite the caller can see that targets the asset, with its latest run."""

62	class AssetSummaryRead(ApiModel):
63	    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset, carrying **two orthogonal health
64	    axes** (#803) the UI renders separately:
65	
66	    - *Suite health* (data quality) — `worst_severity` / `checks_*` over the
67	      **evaluated** checks of the caller-visible composing suites' latest runs;
68	      `worst_severity` is null when all passed or nothing has run. Operational
69	      results never rank here.
70	    - *Connection health* (reachability) — `has_operational_error` / `has_skip`
71	      (plus the execution states below): could DataQ execute against the
72	      datasource at all? Derived from the recorded runs only — no connection-probe
73	      polling loop.
74	    """
75	
76	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
77	
78	    id: uuid.UUID
79	    namespace: str
80	    # Null = a REDACTED browse row (#920): the asset is monitored solely by suites
81	    # outside the caller's grants; browse includes it anonymously (the tree-level
82	    # #845 rule) — namespace only for placement, every other identity/health field
83	    # withheld, `is_accessible=False`. The detail endpoint keeps 404ing it.
84	    name: str | None
85	    env: str | None
86	    description: str | None
87	    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
88	    last_seen: datetime | None
89	    suite_count: int
90	    worst_severity: str | None
91	    checks_total: int
92	    checks_passed: int
93	    last_run_at: datetime | None
94	    # Latest-run execution states (distinct from check severity): any composing
95	    # suite's latest run `failed` / still `queued`/`running`.
96	    has_failed_run: bool
97	    has_active_run: bool
98	    # Connection health (#803): a failed run OR any `error` result → DataQ could not
99	    # evaluate against the datasource; `skip` → a precondition wasn't met (degraded);
100	    # `cancelled` → the run was killed, so it proves nothing (never rolls up green).
101	    has_cancelled_run: bool
102	    has_operational_error: bool
103	    has_skip: bool
104	    is_accessible: bool = True
105	    # Redacted rows only (#920): the non-leaf path segments for tree placement,
106	    # pre-split server-side (one separator rule for client and server).
107	    name_prefix_segments: list[str] | None = None
108	
109	
110	class LineageNodeRead(ApiModel):
111	    """A lineage neighbour — OpenLineage identity + whether it is monitored. No
112	    run data (blast-radius browse only; ADR 0034 §2).
113	
114	    `depth` is the hop distance from the asset under view (1 = a direct neighbour):
115	    the graph view lays nodes out in hop columns rather than flattening every hop
116	    into one list (#805)."""
117	
118	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
119	
120	    id: uuid.UUID
121	    # Nullable because a neighbour the caller holds no grant for is REDACTED, not
122	    # omitted (#845): the graph still shows that something is downstream — dropping it
123	    # would assert the falsehood "nothing consumes this table" — but its identity is
124	    # withheld, because the asset endpoint 404s it no-leak (ADR 0034 decision 5) and a
125	    # graph that names it would defeat that guarantee one click earlier.
126	    namespace: str | None
127	    name: str | None
128	    env: str | None
129	    # False for a redacted node: whether someone else monitors an asset you cannot see
130	    # is itself a fact about that asset.
131	    is_monitored: bool
132	    depth: int
133	    # False → this node is a redacted placeholder: not nameable, not openable.
134	    is_accessible: bool
135	
136	
137	class LineageEdgeRead(ApiModel):
138	    """One edge of the lineage neighbourhood, `source` (upstream) → `target`
139	    (downstream) asset id. The UI draws exactly these — without them a graph could
140	    only guess which depth-2 node hangs off which depth-1 node (#805).
141	
142	    `columns` is the edge's column-level refinement (#901) where a warehouse source
143	    recorded one — `[upstream_column, downstream_column]` pairs. **Redacted
144	    server-side by the same #845 one-rule as the nodes**: when either endpoint is
145	    outside the caller's grants, `columns` is null and only `column_count` remains —
146	    the UI renders that as a redacted box ("N column links"), because a column name
147	    of an asset you cannot see is schema disclosure. Both null ⇒ the edge simply has
148	    no column grain (table-level source)."""
149	
150	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
151	
152	    source: uuid.UUID
153	    target: uuid.UUID
154	    columns: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None
155	    column_count: int | None = None
156	
157	
158	class LineageSourceHealthRead(ApiModel):
159	    """A lineage-feeding connection whose poll is currently failing (#828).
160	
161	    Present so the UI can never render a clean "no lineage recorded" empty state over a
162	    broken integration — the failure mode that let prod lineage rot for six days behind
163	    an expired credential while the product reported nothing wrong.
164	
165	    `last_error` is a **classified** reason, never raw exception text.
166	    """
167	
168	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
169	
170	    connection_id: uuid.UUID
171	    name: str
172	    type: str
173	    consecutive_failures: int
174	    last_error: str | None = None
175	    last_polled_at: datetime | None = None
176	
177	
178	class WarehouseLineageStatusRead(ApiModel):
179	    """A warehouse-native lineage source (Snowflake / UC) that is degraded or failing —
180	    so the graph can be qualified rather than shown as complete + current (#828, #858).
181	
182	    `tier` is the source that answered (e.g. `snowflake_object_dependencies`);
183	    `degraded_reason` is the "working but coarse" note (view-level only, Enterprise
184	    needed); `last_error` is a **classified** refresh failure. A healthy full-tier source
185	    is not listed.
186	    """
187	
188	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
189	
190	    connection_id: uuid.UUID
191	    name: str
192	    type: str
193	    tier: str | None = None
194	    degraded_reason: str | None = None
195	    last_error: str | None = None
196	    last_refreshed_at: datetime | None = None
197	
198	
199	class AssetDetailRead(ApiModel):
200	    """Asset detail: the summary + per-suite breakdown + upstream/downstream lineage."""
201	
202	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
203	
204	    summary: AssetSummaryRead
205	    suites: list[ComposingSuiteRead]
206	    upstream: list[LineageNodeRead]
207	    downstream: list[LineageNodeRead]
208	    lineage_edges: list[LineageEdgeRead]
209	    # Non-empty ⇒ lineage may be stale/absent for reasons unrelated to this asset.
210	    failing_lineage_sources: list[LineageSourceHealthRead] = Field(default_factory=list)
211	    # Non-empty ⇒ a warehouse lineage source is coarse (degraded tier) or failing.
212	    warehouse_lineage_status: list[WarehouseLineageStatusRead] = Field(default_factory=list)
213	
214	
215	class AssetMetadataUpdate(ApiModel):
216	    """Partial metadata update (workspace-Admin-only). Each field is optional; an
217	    explicit `null` clears it, an omitted field leaves it unchanged — the two are
218	    distinguished via `model_fields_set` at the route so `owner_user_id: null`
219	    means "unassign" rather than "leave as is".
220	
221	    `extra="forbid"`: because omitted-vs-null is semantically load-bearing here, a
222	    typo'd field name (`descripton`) must be a 422, not a silently-ignored no-op."""
223	
224	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
225	
226	    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
227	    # Same cap as suite descriptions (SuiteCreate).
228	    description: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=1024)
229	
230	
231	_LIST_LIMIT_DEFAULT = 200
232	_LIST_LIMIT_MAX = 200
233	
234	
235	@router.get("/assets", response_model=list[AssetSummaryRead], summary="List visible assets")
236	def list_assets(
237	    current_user: Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)],
238	    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
239	    limit: int = Query(default=_LIST_LIMIT_DEFAULT, ge=1, le=_LIST_LIMIT_MAX),
240	    offset: int = Query(default=0, ge=0),
241	) -> list[svc.AssetSummary]:
242	    # Visibility derived from suite grants — a workspace-admin sees every asset
243	    # (ADR 0027), everyone else only assets with a suite they can view. Pages are
244	    # a stable (namespace, name) ordering sliced by limit/offset.
245	    return svc.list_visible_assets(
246	        db,
247	        user_id=current_user.id,
248	        include_all=is_workspace_admin(current_user),
249	        limit=limit,
250	        offset=offset,
251	    )
252	
253	
254	@router.get("/assets/{asset_id}", response_model=AssetDetailRead, summary="Get an asset")
255	def get_asset(
256	    asset_id: uuid.UUID,
257	    current_user: Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)],
258	    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
259	) -> svc.AssetDetail:
260	    # Raises AssetNotFoundError (404) for an unknown asset OR one the caller can
261	    # see no composing suite for — existence hidden (no-leak, ADR 0027).
262	    return svc.get_visible_asset(
263	        db, asset_id, user_id=current_user.id, include_all=is_workspace_admin(current_user)
264	    )
265	
266	
267	@router.patch(
268	    "/assets/{asset_id}",
269	    response_model=AssetSummaryRead,
270	    summary="Update asset metadata (workspace-admin only)",
271	)
272	def update_asset(
273	    asset_id: uuid.UUID,
274	    payload: AssetMetadataUpdate,
275	    # Workspace-Admin-only (ADR 0034 §4) — a non-admin gets a real 403.
276	    _admin: Annotated[User, Depends(require_workspace_admin)],
277	    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
278	) -> svc.AssetSummary:
279	    fields = payload.model_fields_set
280	    asset = svc.update_asset_metadata(
281	        db,
282	        asset_id,
283	        owner_user_id=payload.owner_user_id,
284	        description=payload.description,
285	        set_owner="owner_user_id" in fields,
286	        set_description="description" in fields,
287	    )
288	    # Return the refreshed summary (admin sees all → include_all). Never 404s on
289	    # an asset with no composing suites — metadata exists independently of suites.
290	    return svc.summarize_asset(db, asset, user_id=_admin.id, include_all=True)

input.command:
gh issue create --title "assets: workspace-visible identity + workspace-true aggregates — grant boundary moves to suite-derived detail (supersedes #920 redaction)" --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --label enhancement --body "## Summary
Change the asset access model (user decision, 2026-07-18): **asset identity and lineage topology are visible to every authenticated workspace member; aggregate quality verdicts are workspace-true (one number for all viewers); itemized evaluation stays behind ADR 0027 suite grants.**

> What exists and how it connects is workspace knowledge; what we measured about it belongs to the suite's grants — except the aggregate verdict, which is a workspace fact (one truth for everyone).

## Why
- A new member sees only suite-less assets + 🔒 boxes today — a discovery dead-end: they cannot see what tables exist or what's already monitored, so they can't meaningfully target a suite (the bootstrap problem).
- Mature catalog products (DataHub, Collibra, Atlas) all converge on metadata-visible-to-all, data/results-restricted.
- Per-viewer health rollups silently disagree between users about the same table — the exact problem the #889 scorecard flagged (a workspace-true score next to a per-viewer badge would show two verdicts on one page).
- The trust boundary already is workspace membership (single-tenant; connections are already listed unscoped to every member).

## Model
| Layer | Rule |
|---|---|
| Asset identity (name/namespace/env/description/owner/last_seen), tree placement | every member |
| Lineage topology (nodes, edges, **column pairs**) | every member — #845 anonymous nodes + #920 redacted rows/#901 count-only column boxes retire |
| Aggregate rollup (worst_severity, checks, run-state flags, suite_count) | every member, **workspace-true** (computed over ALL composing suites) |
| Composing-suite list on detail | grant-scoped; the rest collapse to a \`restricted_suite_count\` |
| Runs / results / samples / incidents | grant-scoped (ADR 0027 unchanged; suite endpoints keep 404-no-leak) |
| Asset metadata mutation | workspace-admin only (unchanged) |
| Lineage-source health (infra) | every member (stake gate retired — connections are already workspace-listed) |

## Scope
- ADR 0037 (new) + amendment notes on ADR 0027 / ADR 0034 decision 5 (second amendment — supersedes the #845/#846/#920 redaction-for-identity regime)
- Backend: \`asset_view_service\` visibility rewrite (browse full rows for all, workspace-true \`_roll_up\`, detail 200s for every existing asset + \`restricted_suite_count\`, de-redacted lineage nodes/edges), API models
- Frontend: remove restricted rendering (tree/table/graph/column panel), restricted-suite affordance on detail, dashboard Monitored stat = \`suite_count > 0\`
- Tests: rewrite the three-surfaces one-rule test + wire tests to pin the NEW rule
- Related issues to update, not build: #889 (scorecard authz simplifies to workspace-true), #919 (catalog sync becomes the natural v-next), #920 (superseded)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A member with zero grants sees every asset fully named in browse/tree with workspace-true health, and can open any asset detail (identity + lineage + aggregate; suite list filtered, restricted count shown)
- [ ] Lineage graph + column panel show full identity to every member; no redacted/anonymous rendering remains
- [ ] Suite/run/result/sample/incident endpoints unchanged (grant-scoped, 404-no-leak at the suite grain)
- [ ] Three-surfaces test pins browse/detail/graph agreement on the new rule
- [ ] ADR 0037 merged; 0027/0034 amendment notes + index row; feature-matrix updated" 2>&1 | tail -1

content.text: Issue [#923](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/923) filed, branch created. Now the ADR:

input.content:
# ADR 0037 — Workspace-visible asset identity; workspace-true aggregates; grants guard suite-derived detail

- **Status:** Accepted
- **Date:** 2026-07-18
- **Deciders:** @TheurgicDuke771
- **Related:** ADR [0027](0027-suite-permission-model-workspace-admin.md) (the suite grant ladder — **unchanged** by this ADR; gains this ADR as the asset-layer companion), ADR [0033](0033-workspace-roles-rbac.md) (workspace roles — a Viewer is still a member and sees identity like everyone else), ADR [0034](0034-asset-entity-openlineage-identity-lineage-pull.md) (decision 5 — **amended a second time**, superseding the #845/#846/#920 redaction-for-identity regime), ADR [0036](0036-connection-anchored-check-engines.md) §scorecard
- **Issue:** [#923](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/923). Supersedes the identity-redaction halves of [#845](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/845)/[#846](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/846) (anonymous lineage nodes), [#901](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/901) (count-only column boxes) and [#920](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/920) (redacted browse rows). Simplifies [#889](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/889) (scorecard) and unblocks the onboarding story behind [#919](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/919) (catalog sync).

## Context

Until now, asset visibility **derived from suite grants** (ADR 0034 decision 5): an
asset was visible iff the caller could view ≥1 composing suite (or, per the
#845/#846 amendment, had no suites at all), everything else was redacted — anonymous
lineage nodes, count-only column boxes, and (as of #920, one day before this ADR)
locked "🔒 Restricted" browse rows. The health rollup was **per-viewer**, computed
over only the suites the caller's grants covered.

Three forces broke this model:

1. **The bootstrap dead-end.** A newly enrolled member holds no grants, so they see
   only suite-less assets plus a wall of locked boxes. They cannot discover what
   tables exist, what feeds what, or what is already monitored — which is precisely
   the information they need to author their first suite. The browse surface was
   informationally useless to the people who need it most.
2. **Per-viewer aggregates silently disagree.** Two users could look at the same
   asset and see different health verdicts, with nothing indicating either view was
   partial. The scorecard design (#889) hit this head-on: a workspace-true score
   sitting next to a per-viewer badge would render two conflicting verdicts about
   one table on one page. #889 had to invent "redacted count contributions" to
   square it — redaction machinery applied to *arithmetic*.
3. **The boundary protected the wrong thing.** The workspace is single-tenant;
   every member is a colleague authenticated through the workspace IdP, and the
   *connections* list — arguably more sensitive than table names — has been
   workspace-visible to every member since Week 2. Meanwhile the genuinely
   sensitive material (failing-row **samples**, check results, run history, suite
   configuration) was never protected by asset redaction at all — it lives behind
   the suite grants, where it stays.

This is also where mature catalog products (DataHub, Collibra, Atlas) converged:
**metadata visible to all, data and results restricted.**

## Decision

One rule, three layers:

> **What exists and how it connects is workspace knowledge. The aggregate verdict
> is a workspace fact — one truth for every viewer. What was measured, by whom,
> and on which rows belongs to the suite's grants.**

| Layer | Contents | Visibility |
|---|---|---|
| **Identity & topology** | asset name, namespace, env, description, owner, `last_seen`, tree placement; lineage nodes, edges, **column-level pairs**; `is_monitored`; lineage-source health (dbt poll / warehouse tier advisories) | **Every authenticated member** |
| **Aggregate verdicts** | `worst_severity`, `checks_total`/`checks_passed`, run-state flags (`has_failed_run` etc.), `last_run_at`, `suite_count`; (future) the #889 scorecard's per-dimension coverage + scores | **Every member — workspace-true**: computed over **all** composing suites, identical for every viewer |
| **Itemized evaluation** | composing-suite names + per-suite runs on the asset page, suite/check configuration, run detail, results, failing-row samples, incidents | **Grant-scoped** (ADR 0027 ladder, unchanged). Non-visible suites collapse to a `restricted_suite_count` — a count, never names |

Concretely:

- **Browse** returns a full row for every asset to every member. The #920 redacted
  row (`name: null`, `name_prefix_segments`, `is_accessible`) is removed from the
  contract, not just unused.
- **The asset detail endpoint 200s for every existing asset.** The 404-no-leak rule
  *retires at the asset grain* and lives on unchanged at the suite grain: suite,
  run, result and incident endpoints still 404 what your grants don't cover, and
  the asset page's suite table lists only your suites plus
  `restricted_suite_count`.
- **The lineage graph names every node** and column-level pairs are shown to every
  member. Column names are schema metadata — identity, not measurement. The #845
  anonymous node and the #901 count-only column box are removed.
- **Health/scorecard aggregation is workspace-true.** `_roll_up` computes over all
  composing suites regardless of the caller. The #889 scorecard inherits this: the
  numbers are public, the drill-down is granted.
- **Lineage-source health advisories** (failing dbt polls, degraded warehouse
  tiers) are shown to every member. The previous "stake gate" guarded connection
  names that every member can already read off `GET /connections`; it gated
  nothing.
- **Asset metadata mutation** (owner, description) stays workspace-Admin-only.
- **Incidents stay grant-scoped.** An incident carries failure evidence — itemized
  measurement, not identity.

## What this deliberately discloses

Named explicitly so nobody discovers it by surprise:

- **Existence + naming of every table/file an asset row exists for**, to every
  member — including tables monitored exclusively by someone else's suites.
- **That an asset is monitored, and by how many suites** — the discovery signal a
  new user needs ("this table matters"); suite *names* stay behind grants.
- **The workspace-true verdict** — a member can see that a table they hold no
  grants on is failing critically. That is the point: a data consumer deciding
  whether to trust a table needs the verdict most when they are not the one
  monitoring it. What they cannot see is which check, which rows, or any sample
  values.

Deployments that need identity itself compartmentalized inside one workspace
(multi-team regulated tenancy) are out of scope for the single-tenant product; that
would be a future per-workspace strictness mode and warrants its own ADR — a
config knob was considered and rejected below.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- A day-one member can browse the full estate, read lineage end-to-end, see what's
  monitored and how it's doing, and pick an unmonitored table to target — the
  onboarding path exists.
- One verdict per asset. Browse, detail, graph, dashboard and (future) scorecard
  can never disagree with each other *or between viewers* about aggregate state.
- The redaction machinery — `_redacted_summary`, `_accessible_asset_ids`,
  anonymous nodes, count-only column boxes, locked tree leaves, and their wire
  fields — is deleted, not maintained. The #889 scorecard sheds its
  redacted-count-contributions mechanism before it was ever built.
- #919 (warehouse catalog sync) becomes a pure inventory feature: synced-but-
  unmonitored tables surface as ordinary public identity rows.

**Costs / risks (accepted)**
- **A reversal one day after #920 shipped.** The redacted-browse-row build taught
  us the layering was wrong (its live test made a new member's view visibly
  useless); its wire tests convert into pinning the new rule. Recorded, not
  hidden.
- **Aggregate verdicts leak coarse information about other teams' monitoring**
  (see the disclosure list). Accepted for a single-tenant workspace.
- **`suite_count` + workspace-true totals let any member infer activity levels**
  on tables they don't monitor. Accepted — same class as the above.

## Alternatives considered

- **Status quo (derived visibility + redaction).** Rejected: the bootstrap
  dead-end is structural, and per-viewer aggregates are quietly inconsistent.
- **Per-workspace strictness toggle (`ASSET_VISIBILITY=workspace|granted`).**
  Rejected for now: a config axis on an authz rule doubles every visibility test
  and blurs the one-rule guarantee. If a real deployment needs the strict mode,
  that decision gets its own ADR with the demand in hand.
- **Public identity but per-viewer aggregates.** Rejected: recreates the
  two-verdicts-on-one-page inconsistency the scorecard surfaced (#889), and health
  derived from "the suites you happen to see" is an unlabeled partial truth.
- **Asset-level ACLs.** Rejected in ADR 0034 and stays rejected — this ADR removes
  the need for an asset-grant concept rather than adding one.

## Test contract

The three-surfaces agreement test
(`backend/tests/services/test_lineage_authz_redaction.py`) pins the new rule: for
any (asset × caller), browse, detail and graph agree that identity + aggregates
are present and identical for every member, and that itemized suite data appears
iff the ADR 0027 ladder grants it. The suite-grain 404-no-leak keeps its own
tests untouched.

input.old_string:
"""Read-only asset view — the browse/reason surface over `assets` (ADR 0034, #760).

Assets are what users *reason about*; suites remain how checks *execute* (ADR 0034
guiding principle). This module aggregates, per asset, the suites that target it +
their latest run health + the lineage neighbourhood, for the `/assets` API.

**Authz is derived, never granted (ADR 0034 decision 5 / ADR 0027).** An asset is
visible iff the caller can `view` ≥1 suite mapped to it (`suites.asset_id`) **or the
asset has no suites at all**; the aggregation is filtered to *only* the suites the
caller's grants cover; a workspace-admin sees every suite (`include_all`). An asset
whose suites the caller can all not see is 404-no-leak (the API layer raises
`AssetNotFoundError`). This reuses `suite_service.accessible_suite_ids` verbatim, so
the visibility rule has a single source of truth and can never drift from the
suites/runs surfaces.

**The suite-less clause is an ADR 0034 amendment (#845/#846).** Redaction protects a
*grant*; an asset nobody has granted is protected by nothing — a suite-less asset (a
raw source table, an unmonitored mart, an asset whose last suite was deleted, its runs
cascading with it per #540) has no runs, results or samples behind it. Withholding only
its *name*, while the lineage graph reveals its existence anyway, bought nothing and
cost a great deal: it made browse disagree with the detail endpoint about what exists,
and it would have painted every raw upstream "🔒 Restricted" — which isn't restricted,
it's merely unmonitored.

Asset-metadata mutation (owner, description) is workspace-Admin-only — enforced at
the API layer (`require_workspace_admin`), not here; `update_asset_metadata` is the
plain persistence half.

**Lineage nodes ARE authz-filtered — but redacted, never dropped (#845).** The walk
itself is unscoped, because blast radius is the point (ADR 0034 §2) and a table's
consumers do not stop existing because you can't see them. A neighbour behind the grant
boundary is therefore returned as an **anonymous** node (id + depth only; no name,
namespace, env, or monitored flag) rather than named — a graph that named it would
defeat the no-leak 404 one click earlier, which is precisely what it used to do — and
rather than removed, since removing it would assert "nothing consumes this table".
"""
input.new_string:
"""Read-only asset view — the browse/reason surface over `assets` (ADR 0034, #760).

Assets are what users *reason about*; suites remain how checks *execute* (ADR 0034
guiding principle). This module aggregates, per asset, the suites that target it +
their latest run health + the lineage neighbourhood, for the `/assets` API.

**The ADR 0037 three-layer rule (supersedes ADR 0034 decision 5 + #845/#846/#920):**

- **Identity & topology are workspace knowledge.** Every authenticated member sees
  every asset's full identity (name, namespace, env, description, owner,
  ``last_seen``) and the full lineage neighbourhood — named nodes, real edges, and
  column-level pairs. Nothing here is redacted, anonymized, or 404'd: the detail
  endpoint opens for every existing asset, and only a truly unknown id 404s.
- **Aggregate verdicts are workspace-true.** The health rollup (``worst_severity``,
  check counts, run-state flags, ``suite_count``) is computed over **all** composing
  suites for **every** viewer — one truth per asset, never a per-viewer partial that
  silently disagrees between users (#889's two-verdicts-on-one-page problem).
- **Itemized evaluation stays behind the ADR 0027 suite grants.** The per-suite
  breakdown on the detail page lists only suites the caller can view
  (`suite_service.accessible_suite_ids` — the single source of truth, so this can
  never drift from the suites/runs surfaces); the rest collapse to
  ``restricted_suite_count`` — a count, never names. Suite/run/result/sample/incident
  endpoints keep their own grant scoping and 404-no-leak at the suite grain.

Asset-metadata mutation (owner, description) is workspace-Admin-only — enforced at
the API layer (`require_workspace_admin`), not here; `update_asset_metadata` is the
plain persistence half.
"""

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AssetSummary:
    """List-row aggregation for one visible asset.

    **Two orthogonal health axes (#803).** The old single "health" conflated them:

    - *Suite health* (data quality, ADR 0005) — ``worst_severity`` over the
      **evaluated** checks, plus ``checks_total``/``checks_passed``. Operational
      results never rank here, so a datasource DataQ couldn't reach reads as "no
      data", never as a green "passing" nor as a red data failure.
    - *Connection health* (reachability) — ``has_operational_error`` /
      ``has_skip``: could DataQ execute against the datasource at all? Derived
      **purely from the runs already recorded** (a latest run that `failed`, or
      any ``error``/``skip`` result on one) — there is no connection-probe polling
      loop behind this, by design.
    """

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str
    # ``None`` = a REDACTED row (#920): the asset exists, is monitored by suites the
    # caller holds no grant on, and browse includes it as an anonymous entry rather
    # than omitting it — the tree-level twin of the lineage graph's #845 rule
    # (omission asserts "this schema holds nothing else"). The namespace stays (the
    # tree needs the placement — a deliberate, user-directed disclosure); the NAME
    # is the protected fact, along with env/description/owner and every health
    # field (forced to their empty defaults — a hidden asset's health is itself a
    # fact about it). The detail endpoint keeps 404ing these no-leak.
    name: str | None
    env: str | None
    description: str | None
    owner_user_id: uuid.UUID | None
    # None on redacted rows (#920) — liveness cadence is a fact about the asset.
    last_seen: datetime | None
    suite_count: int

input.old_string:
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class LineageNode:
    """A lineage neighbour — enough to render, no run data (ADR 0034 §2).

    ``depth`` is the hop distance from the asset under view (1 = a direct
    neighbour), which is what lets the UI lay the graph out in columns (#805)
    instead of flattening every hop into one list.

    **A neighbour outside the caller's grants is REDACTED, not omitted (#845).** The
    lineage walk is not authz-scoped — it can't be, because the graph's job is blast
    radius, and a table's real consumers do not stop existing because you can't see
    them. But the asset endpoint 404s those assets *no-leak* (ADR 0034 decision 5), so
    handing their ``name``/``namespace``/``env`` to a non-grantee through the graph
    would defeat that guarantee one click earlier — and did.

    So an inaccessible neighbour keeps only ``id`` (an opaque UUID; the edges need it
    to draw the shape) and ``depth``: identity fields are ``None`` and ``is_monitored``
    is forced ``False``. The user still learns that *something* is downstream — which
    keeps the blast radius honest instead of asserting the confident falsehood
    "nothing consumes this table" (the #828/#823 lesson: never fix a leak by shipping a
    lie). What they don't learn is *what* it is.

    Redaction is done **here, server-side**: a name that is hidden in CSS has still
    crossed the wire.
    """

    id: uuid.UUID
    namespace: str | None
    name: str | None
    env: str | None
    is_monitored: bool

def _suite_outcomes(
    suites: list[Suite],
    latest_runs: dict[uuid.UUID, Run],
    outcomes: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[int, int, str | None]],
    op_flags: dict[uuid.UUID, tuple[bool, bool]],
) -> dict[uuid.UUID, list[RunOutcome]]:
    """One ``RunOutcome`` per suite (empty for a never-run suite), grouped by
    asset — the workspace-true aggregation input (ADR 0037): EVERY composing
    suite contributes, independent of any caller's grants."""
    by_asset: dict[uuid.UUID, list[RunOutcome]] = defaultdict(list)
    for suite in suites:
        assert suite.asset_id is not None  # callers filter on asset_id
        run = latest_runs.get(suite.id)
        outcome = outcomes.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
        flags = op_flags.get(run.id) if run is not None else None
        by_asset[suite.asset_id].append(_run_outcome(run, outcome, flags))
    return by_asset

def list_visible_assets(
    session: Session,
    *,
    user_id: uuid.UUID,
    include_all: bool = False,
    limit: int = 200,
    offset: int = 0,
) -> list[AssetSummary]:
    """Assets the caller can see (via ≥1 accessible composing suite), sorted by
    ``(namespace, name)`` and paginated with ``limit``/``offset``.

    Pagination is applied at the SQL level over the *asset* page (not the suite
    rows), so a page is a stable, deterministic slice regardless of how many
    suites compose each asset. Aggregation is filtered to the caller's grants: a
    partial-grant caller sees the asset but only the suites/runs they can view
    roll up into its health."""
    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
    # Browse shows what actually exists — ALL of it (#846, extended by #920):
    #
    # - assets whose suites you can view → full rows;
    # - suite-less assets → full rows (#846 — redaction protects a grant, and an
    #   asset nobody granted is protected by nothing);
    # - assets monitored ONLY by suites you can't see → **REDACTED rows** (#920,
    #   user-directed): omitting them asserted "this schema holds nothing else" —
    #   the same falsehood the lineage graph's #845 rule exists to prevent, one
    #   surface over. The row keeps id + placement and nothing else; the detail
    #   endpoint keeps 404ing it.
    #
    # Accessibility is derived by `_accessible_asset_ids` — the SAME helper the
    # lineage graph redacts with (#911-review: a second SQL encoding of the rule
    # here would be the #845/#847 drift class reborn). Ordering note: redacted rows
    # sort by their true (hidden) name — a weak alphabetical-position signal,
    # accepted with the placement disclosure.
    assets = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Asset).order_by(Asset.namespace, Asset.name).limit(limit).offset(offset)
        )
    )
    if not assets:
        return []
    page_ids = [a.id for a in assets]
    open_ids = _accessible_asset_ids(
        session,
        page_ids,
        user_id=user_id,
        include_all=include_all,
        has_suite=_monitored_ids(session, page_ids),
    )

    suites = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Suite)
            .where(Suite.asset_id.in_(list(open_ids)), Suite.id.in_(accessible))
            .order_by(Suite.name)
        )
    )
    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)

    suites_by_asset: dict[uuid.UUID, list[Suite]] = defaultdict(list)
    for suite in suites:
        assert suite.asset_id is not None  # filtered in the query
        suites_by_asset[suite.asset_id].append(suite)

    return [
        (
            _roll_up(
                asset,
                _composing_suites(
                    suites_by_asset.get(asset.id, []), levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags
                ),
            )
            if asset.id in open_ids
            else _redacted_summary(asset)
        )
        for asset in assets
    ]


def _redacted_summary(asset: Asset) -> AssetSummary:
    """A #920 redacted browse row: id + namespace + the PARENT path only — the leaf
    name is the protected fact, along with env/description/owner and every health
    axis (empty defaults — a hidden asset's health, monitoredness, and suite count
    are facts about it).

    ``name_prefix_segments`` (db/schema or folder path, leaf stripped, ALREADY
    segmented server-side with the same separator rule the tree uses) is a
    deliberate, user-directed disclosure: the tree places the 🔒 row inside its
    real group (`DATAQ_DB → ANALYTICS → Restricted`) — the same placement the
    lineage graph already reveals for any redacted node connected by an edge.
    Shipping segments, not a joined string, keeps one segmentation rule: a
    re-split client-side would re-detect the separator on the PREFIX (which can
    differ from the full name's — e.g. a dotted directory under a slashed path)
    and file the locked row in a fabricated group."""
    sep = "/" if "/" in asset.name else "."
    segments = [part for part in asset.name.split(sep) if part]
    return AssetSummary(
        id=asset.id,
        namespace=asset.namespace,
        name=None,
        name_prefix_segments=segments[:-1] or None,
        env=None,
        description=None,
        owner_user_id=None,
        # Liveness is a fact about the hidden asset too (watching it tick reveals
        # the pipeline's cadence) — withheld like everything else (#921 review).
        last_seen=None,
        suite_count=0,
        worst_severity=None,
        checks_total=0,
        checks_passed=0,
        last_run_at=None,
        is_accessible=False,
    )

def list_visible_assets(
    session: Session,
    *,
    limit: int = 200,
    offset: int = 0,
) -> list[AssetSummary]:
    """Every asset, fully identified, sorted by ``(namespace, name)`` and paginated
    with ``limit``/``offset`` — identical output for every caller (ADR 0037), which
    is why this takes no user: identity is workspace knowledge and the rollup is
    workspace-true (aggregated over ALL composing suites, never grant-filtered).

    Pagination is applied at the SQL level over the *asset* page (not the suite
    rows), so a page is a stable, deterministic slice regardless of how many
    suites compose each asset."""
    assets = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Asset).order_by(Asset.namespace, Asset.name).limit(limit).offset(offset)
        )
    )
    if not assets:
        return []
    page_ids = [a.id for a in assets]
    suites = list(session.scalars(select(Suite).where(Suite.asset_id.in_(page_ids))))
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
    by_asset = _suite_outcomes(suites, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)
    return [_roll_up(asset, by_asset.get(asset.id, [])) for asset in assets]

439	) -> AssetDetail:
440	    """One asset's detail (aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage).
441	
442	    Raises `AssetNotFoundError` (404) if the asset does not exist or the caller can
443	    view no suite targeting it — the two are indistinguishable (no-leak). A
444	    workspace-admin (``include_all``) sees every asset, **including suite-less
445	    orphans** (e.g. a lineage-only node, or an asset whose last composing suite was
446	    deleted) — for them only a truly unknown id 404s."""
447	    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
448	    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
449	    suites = list(
450	        session.scalars(
451	            select(Suite)
452	            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset_id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
453	            .order_by(Suite.name)
454	        )
455	    )
456	    # No-leak: an asset whose suites the caller can ALL not see is indistinguishable from
457	    # one that doesn't exist. That is the grant boundary, and it stays closed.
458	    #
459	    # A **suite-less** asset is not behind that boundary (ADR 0034 amendment, #845/#846):
460	    # no suites means no grant to withhold and nothing behind it — no runs, no results, no
461	    # samples. It opens to an honest, empty page (identity + lineage, no health). Hiding it
462	    # only produced the dead link that surfaced #845: the lineage graph must draw it (else
463	    # the graph claims the table feeds nothing), and a node the graph draws must be a node
464	    # the endpoint opens.
465	    if asset is None:
466	        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
467	    if not suites and not include_all and _monitored_ids(session, [asset_id]):
468	        # It HAS suites — the caller simply can't view any of them. 404, no-leak.
469	        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
470	
471	    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
472	    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
473	    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
474	    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
475	    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
476	    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)
477	
478	    summary = _roll_up(asset, composing)
479	    graph = lineage_neighbourhood(session, asset_id)
480	    neighbour_ids = [a.id for a, _ in graph.upstream] + [a.id for a, _ in graph.downstream]
481	    # ONE lookup of "which of these assets has any suite" — it answers both questions
482	    # below (is it monitored? could a grant even exist for it?), which are the same fact.
483	    has_suite = _monitored_ids(session, neighbour_ids)
484	    # The lineage walk is not authz-scoped (blast radius must stay true), so the caller's
485	    # own visibility is applied HERE, at the boundary — a neighbour they hold no grant for
486	    # is redacted to an anonymous node rather than handed over (#845).
487	    accessible_assets = _accessible_asset_ids(
488	        session,
489	        neighbour_ids,
490	        user_id=user_id,
491	        include_all=include_all,
492	        has_suite=has_suite,
493	    )
494	    return AssetDetail(
