) -> AssetDetail:
    """One asset's detail (aggregation + per-suite breakdown + lineage).

    Raises `AssetNotFoundError` (404) if the asset does not exist or the caller can
    view no suite targeting it — the two are indistinguishable (no-leak). A
    workspace-admin (``include_all``) sees every asset, **including suite-less
    orphans** (e.g. a lineage-only node, or an asset whose last composing suite was
    deleted) — for them only a truly unknown id 404s."""
    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
    suites = list(
        session.scalars(
            select(Suite)
            .where(Suite.asset_id == asset_id, Suite.id.in_(accessible))
            .order_by(Suite.name)
        )
    )
    # No-leak: an asset whose suites the caller can ALL not see is indistinguishable from
    # one that doesn't exist. That is the grant boundary, and it stays closed.
    #
    # A **suite-less** asset is not behind that boundary (ADR 0034 amendment, #845/#846):
    # no suites means no grant to withhold and nothing behind it — no runs, no results, no
    # samples. It opens to an honest, empty page (identity + lineage, no health). Hiding it
    # only produced the dead link that surfaced #845: the lineage graph must draw it (else
    # the graph claims the table feeds nothing), and a node the graph draws must be a node
    # the endpoint opens.
    if asset is None:
        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
    if not suites and not include_all and _monitored_ids(session, [asset_id]):
        # It HAS suites — the caller simply can't view any of them. 404, no-leak.
        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})

    levels = effective_permissions(session, suites, user_id)
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
    composing = _composing_suites(suites, levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)

    summary = _roll_up(asset, composing)
    graph = lineage_neighbourhood(session, asset_id)
    neighbour_ids = [a.id for a, _ in graph.upstream] + [a.id for a, _ in graph.downstream]
    # ONE lookup of "which of these assets has any suite" — it answers both questions
    # below (is it monitored? could a grant even exist for it?), which are the same fact.
    has_suite = _monitored_ids(session, neighbour_ids)
    # The lineage walk is not authz-scoped (blast radius must stay true), so the caller's
    # own visibility is applied HERE, at the boundary — a neighbour they hold no grant for
    # is redacted to an anonymous node rather than handed over (#845).
    accessible_assets = _accessible_asset_ids(
        session,
        neighbour_ids,
        user_id=user_id,
        include_all=include_all,
        has_suite=has_suite,
    )
    return AssetDetail(
        summary=summary,
        suites=composing,
        upstream=_lineage_nodes(graph.upstream, has_suite, accessible_assets),
        downstream=_lineage_nodes(graph.downstream, has_suite, accessible_assets),
        # The viewed asset is accessible by definition (this endpoint already authz'd
        # it); neighbours use the same accessibility set the node redaction derives
        # from, so the edge-level and node-level redaction can never disagree (#845).
        lineage_edges=_lineage_edge_refs(
            session, graph.edges, accessible={asset_id} | accessible_assets
        ),
        # Lineage-source health names ORCHESTRATION CONNECTIONS (name, type, classified
        # poll error) — infrastructure information, not asset information. It is shown to
        # a caller with a stake in this asset (≥1 suite on it) or an admin; a caller who
        # merely reached a suite-less asset through browse has no stake and is not handed
        # the workspace's connection inventory. Without this gate, the #846 visibility
        # widening would have quietly widened an infra disclosure too (#848 review).
        failing_lineage_sources=(
            failing_lineage_sources(session) if (suites or include_all) else []
        ),
        # Same stake gate as failing_lineage_sources — it names workspace connections
        # (infra), so only a caller with a suite on this asset (or an admin) sees it.
        warehouse_lineage_status=(
            warehouse_lineage_status(session) if (suites or include_all) else []
        ),
    )

def get_visible_asset(
    session: Session, asset_id: uuid.UUID, *, user_id: uuid.UUID, include_all: bool = False
) -> AssetDetail:
    """One asset's detail (workspace-true aggregation + the caller's per-suite
    breakdown + lineage). Opens for **every** member (ADR 0037) — only a truly
    unknown id raises `AssetNotFoundError` (404).

    The caller shapes exactly one thing: which composing suites are *listed*
    (their grants; ``include_all`` for a workspace-admin). Suites outside the
    grants still roll into the summary — workspace-true, one verdict for every
    viewer — but surface only as ``restricted_suite_count``: a count, never
    names (suite names can reveal intent; the ADR 0027 boundary lives at the
    suite grain, where its 404-no-leak is intact)."""
    asset = session.get(Asset, asset_id)
    if asset is None:
        raise AssetNotFoundError("asset not found", detail={"asset_id": str(asset_id)})
    all_suites = list(
        session.scalars(select(Suite).where(Suite.asset_id == asset_id).order_by(Suite.name))
    )
    accessible = accessible_suite_ids(user_id, include_all=include_all)
    visible = [s for s in all_suites if s.id in accessible]

    levels = effective_permissions(session, visible, user_id)
    # Latest runs / outcomes over ALL composing suites — the workspace-true rollup
    # input; the grant-filtered `visible` list reuses the same lookups.
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in all_suites])
    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
    composing = _composing_suites(visible, levels, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)
    by_asset = _suite_outcomes(all_suites, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)

    summary = _roll_up(asset, by_asset.get(asset_id, []))
    graph = lineage_neighbourhood(session, asset_id)
    neighbour_ids = [a.id for a, _ in graph.upstream] + [a.id for a, _ in graph.downstream]
    # One grouped lookup of "which of these assets has any suite" — the structural
    # `is_monitored` fact on the nodes.
    has_suite = _monitored_ids(session, neighbour_ids)
    return AssetDetail(
        summary=summary,
        suites=composing,
        restricted_suite_count=len(all_suites) - len(composing),
        upstream=_lineage_nodes(graph.upstream, has_suite),
        downstream=_lineage_nodes(graph.downstream, has_suite),
        lineage_edges=_lineage_edge_refs(session, graph.edges),
        # Source-health advisories name workspace connections — which every member
        # can already read off `GET /connections` (unscoped since Week 2), so there
        # is nothing to gate (ADR 0037 retires the former stake gate). What matters
        # is the #828 rule: never render a confident empty graph over a broken feed.
        failing_lineage_sources=failing_lineage_sources(session),
        warehouse_lineage_status=warehouse_lineage_status(session),
    )

def summarize_asset(session: Session, asset: Asset) -> AssetSummary:
    """Roll one already-loaded asset up into its list-row summary — workspace-true
    (ADR 0037), so it takes no user. An asset with zero suites rolls up to an
    empty (no-run) health summary. Used by the admin PATCH response, where the
    asset need not have suites to have metadata."""
    suites = list(session.scalars(select(Suite).where(Suite.asset_id == asset.id)))
    latest_runs = _latest_run_per_suite(session, [s.id for s in suites])
    run_ids = [r.id for r in latest_runs.values()]
    outcomes = check_outcome_counts(session, run_ids)
    op_flags = operational_result_flags(session, run_ids)
    by_asset = _suite_outcomes(suites, latest_runs, outcomes, op_flags)
    return _roll_up(asset, by_asset.get(asset.id, []))

    would render "🔒 Restricted" to a non-admin, when it isn't restricted at all — it's
    merely unmonitored. Lineage would be a wall of locked boxes.

    assets a grant could exist for. It is passed in rather than re-queried because the
    caller has already computed it (it is the same set the ``is_monitored`` flag reads),

    fact about that asset.

            id=a.id,
            namespace=a.namespace if a.id in accessible else None,
            name=a.name if a.id in accessible else None,
            env=a.env if a.id in accessible else None,
            is_monitored=a.id in monitored and a.id in accessible,
            depth=depth,
            is_accessible=a.id in accessible,
        )
        for a, depth in assets
    ]

def _lineage_nodes(
    assets: list[tuple[Asset, int]],
    monitored: set[uuid.UUID],
) -> list[LineageNode]:
    """Map reachable lineage assets (+ their hop depth) to render-only nodes —
    fully named for every member (ADR 0037); ``is_monitored`` is the true
    structural fact."""
    return [
        LineageNode(
            id=a.id,
            namespace=a.namespace,
            name=a.name,
            env=a.env,
            is_monitored=a.id in monitored,
            depth=depth,
        )
        for a, depth in assets
    ]

    """List-row aggregation for one asset — **workspace-true** (ADR 0037): every
    field is identical for every viewer, aggregated over ALL composing suites.
    Carries **two orthogonal health axes** (#803) the UI renders separately:

    - *Suite health* (data quality) — `worst_severity` / `checks_*` over the
      **evaluated** checks of the composing suites' latest runs;

      results never rank here.

      (plus the execution states below): could DataQ execute against the
      datasource at all? Derived from the recorded runs only — no connection-probe

class LineageNodeRead(ApiModel):
    """A lineage neighbour — OpenLineage identity + whether it is monitored. No
    run data (blast-radius browse only; ADR 0034 §2).

    `depth` is the hop distance from the asset under view (1 = a direct neighbour):
    the graph view lays nodes out in hop columns rather than flattening every hop
    into one list (#805)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    id: uuid.UUID
    # Nullable because a neighbour the caller holds no grant for is REDACTED, not
    # omitted (#845): the graph still shows that something is downstream — dropping it
    # would assert the falsehood "nothing consumes this table" — but its identity is
    # withheld, because the asset endpoint 404s it no-leak (ADR 0034 decision 5) and a
    # graph that names it would defeat that guarantee one click earlier.
    namespace: str | None
    name: str | None
    env: str | None
    # False for a redacted node: whether someone else monitors an asset you cannot see
    # is itself a fact about that asset.
    is_monitored: bool
    depth: int
    # False → this node is a redacted placeholder: not nameable, not openable.
    is_accessible: bool


class LineageEdgeRead(ApiModel):
    """One edge of the lineage neighbourhood, `source` (upstream) → `target`
    (downstream) asset id. The UI draws exactly these — without them a graph could
    only guess which depth-2 node hangs off which depth-1 node (#805).

    `columns` is the edge's column-level refinement (#901) where a warehouse source
    recorded one — `[upstream_column, downstream_column]` pairs. **Redacted
    server-side by the same #845 one-rule as the nodes**: when either endpoint is
    outside the caller's grants, `columns` is null and only `column_count` remains —
    the UI renders that as a redacted box ("N column links"), because a column name
    of an asset you cannot see is schema disclosure. Both null ⇒ the edge simply has
    no column grain (table-level source)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

    source: uuid.UUID
    target: uuid.UUID
    columns: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None
    column_count: int | None = None

1	"""A lineage neighbour outside the caller's grants is REDACTED, not handed over (#845).
2	
3	Found in prod: a non-admin viewing a shared asset saw a downstream mart in the lineage
4	graph, clicked it, and got "Failed to load asset: asset not found". Two defects behind
5	that one click:
6	
7	1. the graph offered a **dead link** — the walk is not authz-scoped, but the asset
8	   endpoint 404s anything outside the caller's grants;
9	2. the far worse one — the graph **defeated the no-leak 404**. ADR 0034 decision 5 says
10	   an asset outside your grants is "404-no-leak", and the endpoint is carefully built so
11	   it cannot confirm such an asset exists. The graph confirmed it anyway, handing over
12	   the name, namespace, env and monitored-status one click earlier.
13	
14	The fix keeps the node (dropping it would assert "nothing consumes this table" — false,
15	and the same confident-empty-state lie #828/#823 were about) but strips its identity
16	**server-side**: a name hidden in CSS has still crossed the wire.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import uuid
22	from typing import Any, cast
23	
24	import pytest
25	
26	from backend.app.db.models import Asset, LineageEdge, Suite, User
27	from backend.app.services import asset_view_service as svc
28	from backend.app.services import share_service, suite_service
29	from backend.app.services.asset_view_service import AssetNotFoundError
30	from backend.tests.services.test_asset_view_service import _conn, _user
31	
32	_SECRET_NAME = "MART_ORDER_REVENUE"
33	_SECRET_NS = "snowflake://acme-secret-account"
34	
35	
36	def _grant(db: Any, suite: Suite, owner: User, target: User) -> None:
37	    """Give ``target`` view on ``suite`` (the ADR-0027 share ladder)."""
38	    share_service.grant_share(
39	        db, suite.id, actor_id=owner.id, target_user_id=target.id, permission="view"
40	    )
41	    db.commit()
42	
43	
44	def _suite_on(db: Any, owner: User, *, table: str) -> Suite:
45	    conn = _conn(db, owner)
46	    suite = suite_service.create_suite(
47	        db,
48	        name=f"S-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
49	        description=None,
50	        connection_id=conn.id,
51	        created_by=owner.id,
52	        target={"table": table},
53	    )
54	    db.commit()
55	    assert suite.asset_id is not None
56	    return suite
57	
58	
59	def _restricted_downstream(db: Any, upstream_asset_id: uuid.UUID) -> Asset:
60	    """A real asset downstream of ``upstream_asset_id``, targeted by a suite the viewer
61	    will NOT be granted — i.e. exactly the prod shape (Order Header → the mart)."""
62	    stranger = _user(db)
63	    mart_suite = _suite_on(db, stranger, table=_SECRET_NAME)
64	    mart = cast(Asset, db.get(Asset, mart_suite.asset_id))
65	    mart.namespace = _SECRET_NS
66	    mart.name = _SECRET_NAME
67	    db.add(
68	        LineageEdge(
69	            upstream_asset_id=upstream_asset_id,
70	            downstream_asset_id=mart.id,
71	            source="dbt",
72	        )
73	    )
74	    db.commit()
75	    return mart
76	
77	
78	@pytest.fixture
79	def scenario(db_session: Any) -> tuple[User, Asset, Asset]:
80	    """Olivia can view ORDER_HEADER (shared with her). A mart she has no grant for sits
81	    downstream of it."""
82	    owner = _user(db_session)
83	    olivia = _user(db_session)
84	    header_suite = _suite_on(db_session, owner, table="ORDER_HEADER")
85	    _grant(db_session, header_suite, owner, olivia)
86	    header = cast(Asset, db_session.get(Asset, header_suite.asset_id))
87	    mart = _restricted_downstream(db_session, header.id)
88	    return olivia, header, mart
89	
90	
91	def test_the_restricted_neighbour_is_still_shown(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
92	    """It must NOT vanish. An empty downstream would tell Olivia "nothing consumes this
93	    table" — false, and the exact confident-empty-state lie #828/#823 were about. She is
94	    entitled to know a consumer exists; that is the blast radius."""
95	    olivia, header, _ = scenario
96	    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=olivia.id)
97	    assert len(detail.downstream) == 1
98	
99	
100	def test_the_restricted_neighbour_carries_no_identity(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
101	    """The load-bearing assertion: the identity never crosses the service boundary."""
102	    olivia, header, _ = scenario
103	    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=olivia.id)
104	    node = detail.downstream[0]
105	
106	    assert node.is_accessible is False
107	    assert node.name is None
108	    assert node.namespace is None
109	    assert node.env is None
110	    # Whether SOMEONE ELSE monitors an asset you can't see is itself a fact about that
111	    # asset — so it is not reported either.
112	    assert node.is_monitored is False
113	    # The whole DTO, stringified: the secret must not survive anywhere in it (a field
114	    # added later that echoes the name would fail here rather than ship).
115	    assert _SECRET_NAME not in str(node)
116	    assert _SECRET_NS not in str(node)
117	
118	
119	def test_the_redacted_node_is_the_one_the_endpoint_refuses(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
120	    """The two rules must agree. If the graph ever offers a node the endpoint 404s, we
121	    are back to the dead link that surfaced this bug — so pin them together."""
122	    olivia, header, mart = scenario
123	    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=olivia.id)
124	    assert detail.downstream[0].id == mart.id  # the id stays: the edges reference it
125	
126	    with pytest.raises(AssetNotFoundError):
127	        svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, mart.id, user_id=olivia.id)
128	
129	
130	def test_the_edge_survives_redaction(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
131	    """The graph's *shape* is what makes the count meaningful — an edge to a redacted
132	    node must still be drawn, or the placeholder floats disconnected."""
133	    olivia, header, mart = scenario
134	    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=olivia.id)
135	    assert (header.id, mart.id) in [(e.source, e.target) for e in detail.lineage_edges]
136	
137	
138	def test_a_grantee_sees_the_neighbour_in_full(db_session: Any) -> None:
139	    """Redaction is per-viewer, not a property of the node: share the mart's suite with
140	    Olivia and the same node comes back named and openable."""
141	    owner = _user(db_session)
142	    olivia = _user(db_session)
143	    header_suite = _suite_on(db_session, owner, table="ORDER_HEADER")
144	    _grant(db_session, header_suite, owner, olivia)
145	    header = cast(Asset, db_session.get(Asset, header_suite.asset_id))
146	
147	    mart_suite = _suite_on(db_session, owner, table=_SECRET_NAME)
148	    mart = cast(Asset, db_session.get(Asset, mart_suite.asset_id))
149	    db_session.add(
150	        LineageEdge(upstream_asset_id=header.id, downstream_asset_id=mart.id, source="dbt")
151	    )
152	    _grant(db_session, mart_suite, owner, olivia)
153	    db_session.commit()
154	
155	    node = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=olivia.id).downstream[0]
156	    assert node.is_accessible is True
157	    assert node.name is not None and _SECRET_NAME in node.name
158	    assert node.is_monitored is True
159	
160	
161	@pytest.mark.parametrize("include_all", [False, True])
162	def test_the_three_surfaces_agree_on_what_is_visible(db_session: Any, include_all: bool) -> None:
163	    """Browse, the detail endpoint, and the lineage graph must derive ACCESSIBILITY from
164	    the SAME rule — over every asset kind. (#920 changed browse's *presentation* of an
165	    inaccessible asset — a redacted row instead of omission — but the rule is one.)
166	
167	    This is the regression that matters. The bug being fixed here *was* a disagreement
168	    between two of these surfaces: the graph offered a node the endpoint refused. They
169	    agree today; nothing but this test stops a future edit to one of the three
170	    (a SQL predicate, an imperative guard, a set expression) from re-opening the gap.
171	    """
172	    owner = _user(db_session)
173	    viewer = _user(db_session)
174	    stranger = _user(db_session)
175	
176	    granted = _suite_on(db_session, owner, table="GRANTED")
177	    _grant(db_session, granted, owner, viewer)
178	    ungranted = _suite_on(db_session, stranger, table="UNGRANTED")  # suites, none viewer's
179	    suiteless = Asset(namespace=_SECRET_NS, name="SUITELESS", env="dev")
180	    db_session.add(suiteless)
181	    db_session.commit()
182	
183	    kinds = {
184	        "granted": cast(uuid.UUID, granted.asset_id),
185	        "ungranted": cast(uuid.UUID, ungranted.asset_id),
186	        "suiteless": suiteless.id,
187	    }
188	    user_id = stranger.id if include_all else viewer.id  # admin identity is irrelevant
189	
190	    rows_by_id = {
191	        row.id: row
192	        for row in svc.list_visible_assets(db_session, user_id=user_id, include_all=include_all)
193	    }
194	    for kind, asset_id in kinds.items():
195	        # 1) the detail endpoint
196	        try:
197	            svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, asset_id, user_id=user_id, include_all=include_all)
198	            openable = True
199	        except AssetNotFoundError:
200	            openable = False
201	        # 2) browse — since #920 EVERY asset is listed; accessibility is the row's
202	        #    redaction state (an ungranted asset appears anonymous, never omitted).
203	        row = rows_by_id.get(asset_id)
204	        assert row is not None, f"{kind}: browse omitted the asset (include_all={include_all})"
205	        browse_accessible = row.is_accessible
206	        # 3) the lineage graph's own accessibility derivation
207	        graph_accessible = asset_id in svc._accessible_asset_ids(
208	            db_session,
209	            [asset_id],
210	            user_id=user_id,
211	            include_all=include_all,
212	            has_suite=svc._monitored_ids(db_session, [asset_id]),
213	        )
214	
215	        expected = include_all or kind != "ungranted"
216	        assert openable is expected, f"{kind}: detail disagrees (include_all={include_all})"
217	        assert (
218	            browse_accessible is expected
219	        ), f"{kind}: browse disagrees (include_all={include_all})"
220	        assert graph_accessible is expected, f"{kind}: graph disagrees (include_all={include_all})"
221	        # The redacted browse row and the redacted graph node tell the same lie-free
222	        # story: existence yes, identity no.
223	        if not expected:
224	            assert row.name is None and row.env is None
225	
226	
227	def test_a_workspace_admin_sees_everything_unredacted(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
228	    """An admin's visibility is workspace-wide (ADR 0027) — redaction must not apply."""
229	    _, header, mart = scenario
230	    admin = _user(db_session)
231	    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=admin.id, include_all=True)
232	    node = detail.downstream[0]
233	    assert node.is_accessible is True
234	    assert node.name == _SECRET_NAME  # the fixture pinned the identity verbatim
235	    assert node.id == mart.id

content.text: This file needs a full rewrite to pin the new rule. Writing it:

input.content:
"""The ADR 0037 three-layer visibility contract, pinned across every surface.

This file used to pin the *redaction* regime (#845/#920: anonymous lineage nodes,
redacted browse rows, 404-no-leak asset detail). ADR 0037 deliberately superseded
that: asset identity and lineage topology are workspace knowledge, aggregate
verdicts are workspace-true, and the grant boundary lives at the suite grain. The
tests here pin the NEW rule with the same rigor the old ones pinned the old one:

1. **Identity is public.** Every member sees every asset fully named — in browse,
   in the detail endpoint (which opens for every existing asset), and in the
   lineage graph (nodes named, column pairs included).
2. **Aggregates are workspace-true.** The summary a non-grantee sees is byte-for-
   byte the summary the admin sees — one verdict per asset, never a per-viewer
   partial.
3. **Items are granted.** The composing-suite list on detail carries only suites
   the caller can view; the rest surface as ``restricted_suite_count`` — a count,
   never names. (Suite/run/result endpoints keep their own 404-no-leak tests.)
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import dataclasses
import uuid
from typing import Any, cast

import pytest

from backend.app.db.models import Asset, LineageEdge, Suite, User
from backend.app.services import asset_view_service as svc
from backend.app.services import share_service, suite_service
from backend.app.services.asset_view_service import AssetNotFoundError
from backend.tests.services.test_asset_view_service import _conn, _user

_SECRET_NAME = "MART_ORDER_REVENUE"
_SECRET_NS = "snowflake://acme-secret-account"


def _grant(db: Any, suite: Suite, owner: User, target: User) -> None:
    """Give ``target`` view on ``suite`` (the ADR-0027 share ladder)."""
    share_service.grant_share(
        db, suite.id, actor_id=owner.id, target_user_id=target.id, permission="view"
    )
    db.commit()


def _suite_on(db: Any, owner: User, *, table: str) -> Suite:
    conn = _conn(db, owner)
    suite = suite_service.create_suite(
        db,
        name=f"S-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
        description=None,
        connection_id=conn.id,
        created_by=owner.id,
        target={"table": table},
    )
    db.commit()
    assert suite.asset_id is not None
    return suite


def _ungranted_downstream(db: Any, upstream_asset_id: uuid.UUID) -> Asset:
    """A real asset downstream of ``upstream_asset_id``, targeted by a suite the
    viewer holds no grant on — the shape the old redaction regime anonymized."""
    stranger = _user(db)
    mart_suite = _suite_on(db, stranger, table=_SECRET_NAME)
    mart = cast(Asset, db.get(Asset, mart_suite.asset_id))
    mart.namespace = _SECRET_NS
    mart.name = _SECRET_NAME
    db.add(
        LineageEdge(
            upstream_asset_id=upstream_asset_id,
            downstream_asset_id=mart.id,
            source="dbt",
            columns=[["order_total", "revenue"]],
        )
    )
    db.commit()
    return mart


@pytest.fixture
def scenario(db_session: Any) -> tuple[User, Asset, Asset]:
    """Olivia can view ORDER_HEADER (shared with her). A mart she has no grant on
    sits downstream of it."""
    owner = _user(db_session)
    olivia = _user(db_session)
    header_suite = _suite_on(db_session, owner, table="ORDER_HEADER")
    _grant(db_session, header_suite, owner, olivia)
    header = cast(Asset, db_session.get(Asset, header_suite.asset_id))
    mart = _ungranted_downstream(db_session, header.id)
    return olivia, header, mart


def test_an_ungranted_neighbour_is_fully_named(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """Layer 1: lineage topology is identity, and identity is workspace knowledge.
    The node arrives named, placed, and honestly flagged as monitored."""
    olivia, header, mart = scenario
    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=olivia.id)
    assert len(detail.downstream) == 1
    node = detail.downstream[0]
    assert node.id == mart.id
    assert node.name == _SECRET_NAME
    assert node.namespace == _SECRET_NS
    assert node.is_monitored is True  # the true structural fact, not forced False


def test_an_ungranted_asset_detail_opens(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """The asset-grain 404-no-leak is retired: every node the graph draws is a node
    the endpoint opens — for every member. Only a truly unknown id 404s."""
    olivia, _, mart = scenario
    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, mart.id, user_id=olivia.id)
    assert detail.summary.name == _SECRET_NAME

    with pytest.raises(AssetNotFoundError):
        svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, uuid.uuid4(), user_id=olivia.id)


def test_column_pairs_are_shown_to_every_member(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """Column names are schema metadata — identity, not measurement. The pairs on
    an edge to an ungranted asset arrive in full (the #901 count-only box retired)."""
    olivia, header, mart = scenario
    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, header.id, user_id=olivia.id)
    edge = next(e for e in detail.lineage_edges if (e.source, e.target) == (header.id, mart.id))
    assert edge.columns == (("order_total", "revenue"),)


def test_the_suite_boundary_holds_on_detail(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """Layer 3: itemized evaluation stays granted. Olivia opens the mart, but its
    composing suite — which she cannot view — is a count, never a name."""
    olivia, _, mart = scenario
    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, mart.id, user_id=olivia.id)
    assert detail.suites == []
    assert detail.restricted_suite_count == 1
    # The workspace-true summary still counts the suite she cannot list.
    assert detail.summary.suite_count == 1


def test_the_summary_is_workspace_true(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """Layer 2: one verdict per asset. A non-grantee, a grantee, and an admin all
    compute byte-identical summaries — a per-viewer partial can never disagree."""
    olivia, _, mart = scenario
    admin = _user(db_session)
    for_olivia = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, mart.id, user_id=olivia.id).summary
    for_admin = svc.get_visible_asset(
        db_session, mart.id, user_id=admin.id, include_all=True
    ).summary
    assert dataclasses.asdict(for_olivia) == dataclasses.asdict(for_admin)


def test_an_admin_sees_the_suite_list_in_full(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """`include_all` (ADR 0027 workspace-admin) lists every composing suite, so
    nothing collapses into the restricted count."""
    _, _, mart = scenario
    admin = _user(db_session)
    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, mart.id, user_id=admin.id, include_all=True)
    assert len(detail.suites) == 1
    assert detail.restricted_suite_count == 0


def test_a_grantee_sees_their_suite_listed(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """The visible/restricted split follows the grant, per suite: granting Olivia
    the mart suite moves it from the count into the named list."""
    olivia, _, mart = scenario
    mart_suite = cast(
        Suite, db_session.scalars(Suite.__table__.select().where(Suite.asset_id == mart.id)).one()
    )
    suite_obj = db_session.get(Suite, mart_suite.id)
    _grant(db_session, suite_obj, db_session.get(User, suite_obj.created_by), olivia)
    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, mart.id, user_id=olivia.id)
    assert [s.suite_id for s in detail.suites] == [suite_obj.id]
    assert detail.restricted_suite_count == 0


def test_the_three_surfaces_agree_identity_is_public(db_session: Any) -> None:
    """Browse, the detail endpoint, and the lineage nodes must all present the SAME
    identity to every caller — over every asset kind (granted / ungranted /
    suite-less). This is the ADR 0037 successor of the old three-surfaces test: the
    axis is no longer per-viewer accessibility (there is none at the asset grain)
    but that no surface withholds or invents identity for any caller."""
    owner = _user(db_session)
    viewer = _user(db_session)
    stranger = _user(db_session)

    granted = _suite_on(db_session, owner, table="GRANTED")
    _grant(db_session, granted, owner, viewer)
    ungranted = _suite_on(db_session, stranger, table="UNGRANTED")
    suiteless = Asset(namespace=_SECRET_NS, name="SUITELESS", env="dev")
    db_session.add(suiteless)
    db_session.commit()

    kinds = {
        "granted": cast(uuid.UUID, granted.asset_id),
        "ungranted": cast(uuid.UUID, ungranted.asset_id),
        "suiteless": suiteless.id,
    }
    expected_visible_suites = {"granted": 1, "ungranted": 0, "suiteless": 0}
    expected_restricted = {"granted": 0, "ungranted": 1, "suiteless": 0}

    rows_by_id = {row.id: row for row in svc.list_visible_assets(db_session)}
    for kind, asset_id in kinds.items():
        # 1) browse: present, named, with the workspace-true suite count.
        row = rows_by_id.get(asset_id)
        assert row is not None, f"{kind}: browse omitted the asset"
        assert row.name, f"{kind}: browse withheld the name"
        # 2) detail: opens for the plain member; identity matches browse; the
        #    itemized split follows the grants.
        detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, asset_id, user_id=viewer.id)
        assert detail.summary.name == row.name
        assert detail.summary.suite_count == row.suite_count
        assert len(detail.suites) == expected_visible_suites[kind], kind
        assert detail.restricted_suite_count == expected_restricted[kind], kind


def test_browse_lists_every_asset_for_a_plain_member(db_session: Any, scenario: Any) -> None:
    """The #920 redacted browse row is gone from the contract: a member with zero
    grants on the mart still gets its full row, with the workspace-true rollup."""
    olivia, _, mart = scenario
    rows = {r.id: r for r in svc.list_visible_assets(db_session)}
    row = rows[mart.id]
    assert row.name == _SECRET_NAME
    assert row.namespace == _SECRET_NS
    assert row.suite_count == 1

def test_an_admin_sees_the_suite_list_in_full(
    db_session: Any, scenario: Any, make_workspace_admin: Any
) -> None:
    """`include_all` (ADR 0027 workspace-admin) lists every composing suite, so
    nothing collapses into the restricted count. The user must be a REAL
    workspace-admin: `effective_permissions` resolves the `admin` label from the
    allowlist independently of `include_all`."""
    _, _, mart = scenario
    admin = _user(db_session)
    make_workspace_admin(admin.email)
    detail = svc.get_visible_asset(db_session, mart.id, user_id=admin.id, include_all=True)
    assert len(detail.suites) == 1
    assert detail.suites[0].my_permission == "admin"
    assert detail.restricted_suite_count == 0

1	"""Asset view API tests against a real Postgres (db_session) via TestClient.
2	
3	The **authz matrix is the point** (ADR 0034 decision 5 / ADR 0027): asset
4	visibility is derived from suite grants, never granted directly. This exercises
5	owner / edit-share / view-share / no-share / workspace-admin — plus partial-grant
6	aggregation filtering and 404-no-leak — end to end through the HTTP surface.
7	
8	The ADR-0033 **Viewer** role is N/A here: it is not built yet (#740-#743 open), so
9	the ladder under test is owner / edit / view / no-share / workspace-admin. When
10	Viewer lands (#741) it caps at `view`, so it will fold into the view-share rows.
11	
12	Skips without TEST_DATABASE_URL (JSONB/UUID need real Postgres).
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import uuid
18	from collections.abc import Iterator
19	from typing import Any
20	
21	import pytest
22	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
23	
24	from backend.app.core.auth import get_current_user
25	from backend.app.db.models import Asset, Check, LineageEdge, Result, Run, Share, Suite, User
26	from backend.app.db.session import get_db
27	from backend.app.main import app
28	from backend.app.services import suite_service
29	
30	# A fully-resolvable snowflake config (account+database+schema) so a suite target
31	# resolves to a first-class asset (ADR 0034). Two suites on this connection with
32	# the same target resolve to the SAME asset.
33	_SF_CONFIG = {"account": "ab12345.eu-west-1", "database": "ANALYTICS", "schema": "PUBLIC"}
34	_ADMIN_EMAIL = "admin@example.com"
35	
36	
37	@pytest.fixture
38	def client(db_session: Any) -> Iterator[TestClient]:
39	    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = lambda: db_session
40	    try:
41	        yield TestClient(app)
42	    finally:
43	        app.dependency_overrides.clear()
44	
45	
46	def _as(user: User) -> None:
47	    app.dependency_overrides[get_current_user] = lambda: user
48	
49	
50	def _user(db_session: Any, email: str) -> User:
51	    u = User(aad_object_id=uuid.uuid4().hex, email=email)
52	    db_session.add(u)
53	    db_session.flush()
54	    return u
55	
56	
57	def _connection(db_session: Any, owner: User) -> Any:
58	    from backend.app.db.models import Connection
59	
60	    conn = Connection(
61	        name=f"sf-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
62	        type="snowflake",
63	        env="dev",
64	        config=_SF_CONFIG,
65	        secret_ref="kv-x",
66	        created_by=owner.id,
67	    )
68	    db_session.add(conn)
69	    db_session.commit()
70	    return conn
71	
72	
73	def _suite(db_session: Any, owner: User, conn: Any, *, name: str, table: str) -> Suite:
74	    """Create a suite via the service so its target resolves to an asset."""
75	    return suite_service.create_suite(
76	        db_session,
77	        name=name,
78	        description=None,
79	        connection_id=conn.id,
80	        created_by=owner.id,
81	        target={"table": table},
82	    )
83	
84	
85	def _seed_run(db_session: Any, suite: Suite, *, status: str = "fail") -> Run:
86	    """One succeeded run on `suite` carrying a single check result of `status`."""
87	    check = Check(
88	        suite_id=suite.id,
89	        name=f"c-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
90	        kind="expectation",
91	        expectation_type="expect_column_values_to_not_be_null",
92	        config={"column": "x"},
93	    )
94	    db_session.add(check)
95	    db_session.flush()
96	    run = Run(suite_id=suite.id, status="succeeded", triggered_by="t", asset_id=suite.asset_id)
97	    db_session.add(run)
98	    db_session.flush()
99	    db_session.add(Result(run_id=run.id, check_id=check.id, status=status))
100	    db_session.commit()
101	    return run
102	
103	
104	def _share(db_session: Any, suite: Suite, user: User, permission: str) -> None:
105	    db_session.add(Share(suite_id=suite.id, user_id=user.id, permission=permission))
106	    db_session.commit()
107

109	# ── the shared world: asset X (2 suites), asset Y (1 suite) ──────────────────

112	@pytest.fixture
113	def world(db_session: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
114	    owner = _user(db_session, "owner@example.com")
115	    conn = _connection(db_session, owner)

117	    s2 = _suite(db_session, owner, conn, name="Orders volume", table="ORDERS")
118	    s3 = _suite(db_session, owner, conn, name="Customers", table="CUSTOMERS")
119	    # s1 + s2 share the ORDERS asset; s3 is a distinct asset.
120	    assert s1.asset_id is not None and s1.asset_id == s2.asset_id
121	    assert s3.asset_id is not None and s3.asset_id != s1.asset_id
122	    _seed_run(db_session, s1, status="fail")
123	    return {
124	        "owner": owner,
125	        "conn": conn,
126	        "s1": s1,
127	        "s2": s2,
128	        "s3": s3,
129	        "asset_x": s1.asset_id,
130	        "asset_y": s3.asset_id,
131	    }

138	    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
139	) -> None:

142	    assert resp.status_code == 200
143	    by_id = {a["id"]: a for a in resp.json()}
144	    assert str(world["asset_x"]) in by_id and str(world["asset_y"]) in by_id
145	    # Asset X composes BOTH orders suites for the owner (full grants).
146	    assert by_id[str(world["asset_x"])]["suite_count"] == 2
147	    assert by_id[str(world["asset_y"])]["suite_count"] == 1
148	    # The failing run rolls up into the asset health.

152	def test_view_share_partial_grant_filters_aggregation(

245	def test_404_no_leak_bodies_identical(client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
246	    """No-leak means indistinguishable: an existing-but-ungranted asset and a
247	    truly unknown id must return the same status AND the same body shape — the
248	    only permitted variation is the probed id echoed back in the detail."""
249	    outsider = _user(client_db(client), "outsider3@example.com")
250	    _as(outsider)
251	    unknown_id = uuid.uuid4()
252	    existing = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_x']}")
253	    unknown = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{unknown_id}")
254	    assert existing.status_code == unknown.status_code == 404
255	
256	    def normalized(resp: Any) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
257	        body: dict[str, Any] = resp.json()
258	        echoed: str = body["error"]["detail"].pop("asset_id")
259	        return echoed, body
260	
261	    existing_echo, existing_body = normalized(existing)
262	    unknown_echo, unknown_body = normalized(unknown)
263	    # The echoed id must be exactly the probed one (no other id leaks) …
264	    assert existing_echo == str(world["asset_x"])
265	    assert unknown_echo == str(unknown_id)
266	    # … and everything else must be byte-identical between the two cases.
267	    assert existing_body == unknown_body

326	def test_redacted_neighbour_identity_never_reaches_the_response_body(
327	    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
328	) -> None:
329	    """The claim #845 makes is that a restricted neighbour's identity **never crosses the
330	    wire** — so assert it at the wire, not at the DTO one layer above it.
331	
332	    `LineageNodeRead` is a separate Pydantic model from the service dataclass. If someone
333	    later re-tightens `name: str` with an empty-string fallback, or a serializer starts
334	    deriving a display name, the leak would ship with a fully green suite unless something
335	    inspects the raw body. This does."""
336	    db = client_db(client)
337	    stranger = _user(db, "stranger2@example.com")
338	    conn = _connection(db, stranger)
339	    secret = _suite(db, stranger, conn, name="Secret mart", table="MART_SECRET_REVENUE")
340	    assert secret.asset_id is not None
341	    secret_asset = db.get(Asset, secret.asset_id)
342	    db.add(
343	        LineageEdge(
344	            upstream_asset_id=world["asset_x"],
345	            downstream_asset_id=secret.asset_id,
346	            source="dbt",
347	        )
348	    )
349	    db.commit()
350	
351	    _as(world["owner"])
352	    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_x']}")
353	    assert resp.status_code == 200
354	
355	    node = next(n for n in resp.json()["downstream"] if n["id"] == str(secret.asset_id))
356	    assert node["is_accessible"] is False
357	    assert node["name"] is None and node["namespace"] is None and node["env"] is None
358	    assert node["is_monitored"] is False
359	    # The raw body — the actual wire. The name must survive nowhere in it.
360	    assert "MART_SECRET_REVENUE" not in resp.text
361	    assert secret_asset is not None and secret_asset.name not in resp.text

364	def test_asset_with_only_unshared_suites_is_404_and_unlisted(
365	    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
366	) -> None:
367	    """The grant boundary that DOES stay closed (#845/#846, amended by #920).
368	
369	    An asset that someone *else* monitors, whose suites the caller cannot view, now
370	    APPEARS in browse — but only as a redacted row (#920: omission asserted "nothing
371	    else exists here"); its identity never crosses, and the detail endpoint is still
372	    404-no-leak. The browse redaction, the graph redaction, and the 404 are one rule —
373	    if they ever disagree the graph offers a dead link again."""
374	    db = client_db(client)

389	def test_orphan_asset_after_composing_suite_deleted(
390	    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any], make_workspace_admin: Any
391	) -> None:
392	    """Deleting an asset's only composing suite (after it ran) orphans the asset. It stays
393	    visible — to **everyone**, not just admins (ADR 0034 amendment, #845/#846) — with an
394	    empty suites list and no health.
395	
396	    This reverses the earlier rule (orphans hidden from non-admins), deliberately. A
397	    suite-less asset has no suites, runs, results or samples behind it — the delete
398	    cascaded all of it (#540) — so there is no grant to protect and nothing to leak but
399	    the *name*, which the lineage graph reveals the existence of regardless. Hiding it

400	    bought no security and cost real correctness: browse and the detail endpoint
401	    disagreed about what existed, and every unmonitored upstream in a lineage graph would
402	    have rendered "restricted" to a non-admin when it is nothing of the sort.
403	
404	    The grant boundary that *does* stay closed is an asset with suites the caller cannot
405	    view — see `test_asset_with_only_unshared_suites_is_404_and_unlisted`."""
406	    db = client_db(client)
407	    _seed_run(db, world["s3"], status="pass")  # the suite has run history
408	    suite_service.delete_suite(db, world["s3"].id)  # cascades runs/results (#540)
409	
410	    _as(world["owner"])
411	    listed = {a["id"] for a in client.get("/api/v1/assets").json()}
412	    assert str(world["asset_y"]) in listed  # browse shows what exists
413	    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_y']}")
414	    assert resp.status_code == 200
415	    body = resp.json()
416	    assert body["suites"] == []
417	    assert body["summary"]["suite_count"] == 0
418	    assert body["summary"]["last_run_at"] is None  # run history died with the suite
419	
420	    admin = _user(db, _ADMIN_EMAIL)
421	    make_workspace_admin(_ADMIN_EMAIL)
422	    _as(admin)

424	    assert admin_body["suites"] == []

472	def test_lineage_cycle_terminates_and_dedupes(client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
473	    """A cycle in `lineage_edges` (X → Y → X) must not hang the BFS; the peer
474	    node appears exactly once per direction (reachable BOTH upstream and
475	    downstream), and the start asset never lists itself."""

479	    db.add(_edge(db, world["asset_y"], world["asset_x"], conn_id))
480	    db.commit()

489	def test_lineage_depth_cap_respected(client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
490	    """A 12-hop downstream chain is cut at the BFS depth cap (10 hops)."""
491	    db = client_db(client)
492	    conn_id = world["conn"].id
493	    chain: list[uuid.UUID] = [world["asset_x"]]
494	    for i in range(12):
495	        node = Asset(namespace="snowflake://ab12345.eu-west-1.aws", name=f"CHAIN.N{i:02d}")
496	        db.add(node)
497	        db.flush()
498	        db.add(_edge(db, chain[-1], node.id, conn_id))
499	        chain.append(node.id)
500	    db.commit()
501	    _as(world["owner"])
502	    body = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_x']}").json()
503	    down_ids = [n["id"] for n in body["downstream"]]
504	    # 10 hops reachable; hops 11 and 12 are beyond the cap.
505	    assert down_ids == [str(a) for a in chain[1:11]]
506	    assert str(chain[11]) not in down_ids and str(chain[12]) not in down_ids

640	def test_column_lineage_redacts_pairs_on_inaccessible_edges(
641	    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
642	) -> None:
643	    """#901 + #845 at the wire: an edge's column pairs are schema disclosure, so they
644	    follow the SAME one-rule as the node identity — a redacted endpoint collapses the
645	    mapping to a count-only box, and the column names never cross the wire. An
646	    accessible edge returns the full pairs (and the same count), so the redacted and
647	    open renderings are provably the same data minus the names."""
648	    db = client_db(client)
649	    stranger = _user(db, "stranger3@example.com")
650	    conn = _connection(db, stranger)
651	    secret = _suite(db, stranger, conn, name="Secret cols", table="MART_SECRET_COLS")
652	    assert secret.asset_id is not None
653	    db.add(
654	        LineageEdge(
655	            upstream_asset_id=world["asset_x"],
656	            downstream_asset_id=secret.asset_id,
657	            source="unity_catalog",
658	            connection_id=conn.id,
659	            columns=[["comment", "sentiment_secret_col"], ["customer_id", "customer_id"]],
660	        )
661	    )
662	    # A fully-visible edge with column data: asset_x -> asset_y (both the owner's).
663	    db.add(
664	        LineageEdge(
665	            upstream_asset_id=world["asset_x"],
666	            downstream_asset_id=world["asset_y"],
667	            source="unity_catalog",
668	            connection_id=conn.id,
669	            columns=[["order_id", "order_id"]],
670	        )
671	    )
672	    db.commit()
673	
674	    _as(world["owner"])
675	    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_x']}")
676	    assert resp.status_code == 200
677	    edges = {(e["source"], e["target"]): e for e in resp.json()["lineage_edges"]}
678	
679	    redacted = edges[(str(world["asset_x"]), str(secret.asset_id))]
680	    assert redacted["columns"] is None
681	    assert redacted["column_count"] == 2
682	    # The wire itself: a hidden column name must survive nowhere in the body.
683	    assert "sentiment_secret_col" not in resp.text
684	
685	    visible = edges[(str(world["asset_x"]), str(world["asset_y"]))]
686	    assert visible["columns"] == [["order_id", "order_id"]]
687	    assert visible["column_count"] == 1
688	
689	    # A table-grain-only edge carries neither field populated.
690	    for e in resp.json()["lineage_edges"]:
691	        if (e["source"], e["target"]) not in (
692	            (str(world["asset_x"]), str(secret.asset_id)),
693	            (str(world["asset_x"]), str(world["asset_y"])),
694	        ):
695	            assert e["columns"] is None and e["column_count"] is None

702	    (what the pre-fix bulk upsert wrote for every no-pairs edge — NOT SQL NULL, so it
703	    passes `is_not(None)` filters) must render as a no-grain edge, never 500.
704	
705	    Written with a literal SQL cast because the fixed ORM type (`none_as_null=True`)
706	    can no longer produce the value — exactly like the 339 rows the first prod
707	    Snowflake refresh left behind."""
708	    from sqlalchemy import text as sql_text
709	
710	    db = client_db(client)
711	    db.add(
712	        LineageEdge(

714	            downstream_asset_id=world["asset_y"],
715	            source="snowflake",
716	        )
717	    )
718	    db.commit()
719	    db.execute(
720	        sql_text(
721	            "UPDATE lineage_edges SET columns = 'null'::jsonb "
722	            "WHERE upstream_asset_id = :up AND source = 'snowflake'"
723	        ),
724	        {"up": str(world["asset_x"])},
725	    )

735	    )

766	) -> None:

768	    caller can't see — as redacted rows (the tree-level #845 rule: omission asserts
769	    "this schema holds nothing else"). Pinned at the wire: only id + namespace +
770	    the PARENT path cross; the leaf name, env, and every health fact stay home."""
771	    db = client_db(client)
772	    stranger = _user(db, "stranger4@example.com")
773	    conn = _connection(db, stranger)
774	    secret = _suite(db, stranger, conn, name="Secret browse", table="MART_SECRET_BROWSE")

787	    assert row["name"] is None and row["env"] is None and row["description"] is None
788	    assert row["owner_user_id"] is None
789	    # Placement is the deliberate disclosure: namespace + the non-leaf path only.
790	    assert row["namespace"] == secret_asset.namespace

792	    assert row["name_prefix_segments"] == secret_asset.name.split(".")[:-1]
793	    assert row["last_seen"] is None  # liveness cadence is a fact about the asset
794	    # Health/monitoredness are facts about the hidden asset — all at empty defaults.
795	    assert row["suite_count"] == 0 and row["checks_total"] == 0
796	    assert row["worst_severity"] is None and row["has_failed_run"] is False
797	    # The wire itself: the leaf name must survive nowhere in the body.

801	    mine = rows[str(world["asset_x"])]
802	    assert mine["is_accessible"] is True and mine["name"] is not None
803	
804	    # A workspace admin still sees the full row (no redaction for include_all).
805	    admin = _user(db, _ADMIN_EMAIL)
806	    make_workspace_admin(_ADMIN_EMAIL)
807	    _as(admin)
808	    admin_rows = {r["id"]: r for r in client.get("/api/v1/assets", params={"limit": 200}).json()}
809	    assert admin_rows[str(secret.asset_id)]["name"] == secret_asset.name

content.text: Now rewriting the nine failing wire tests to pin the new contract:

input.old_string:
"""Asset view API tests against a real Postgres (db_session) via TestClient.

The **authz matrix is the point** (ADR 0034 decision 5 / ADR 0027): asset
visibility is derived from suite grants, never granted directly. This exercises
owner / edit-share / view-share / no-share / workspace-admin — plus partial-grant
aggregation filtering and 404-no-leak — end to end through the HTTP surface.

The ADR-0033 **Viewer** role is N/A here: it is not built yet (#740-#743 open), so
the ladder under test is owner / edit / view / no-share / workspace-admin. When
Viewer lands (#741) it caps at `view`, so it will fold into the view-share rows.

Skips without TEST_DATABASE_URL (JSONB/UUID need real Postgres).
"""
input.new_string:
"""Asset view API tests against a real Postgres (db_session) via TestClient.

The **ADR 0037 three-layer rule is the point**: asset identity + lineage topology
(incl. column pairs) reach every member; the aggregate rollup is workspace-true
(over ALL composing suites — one verdict for every viewer); only the
composing-suite list follows the ADR 0027 grants, the rest collapsing to
`restricted_suite_count`. This exercises owner / edit-share / view-share /
no-share / workspace-admin end to end through the HTTP surface — including that
the suite boundary (names, runs) holds while identity flows.

The ADR-0033 **Viewer** role is N/A here: it is not built yet (#740-#743 open), so
the ladder under test is owner / edit / view / no-share / workspace-admin. When
Viewer lands (#741) it caps at `view`, so it will fold into the view-share rows.

Skips without TEST_DATABASE_URL (JSONB/UUID need real Postgres).
"""

result.output: The file /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/tests/api/test_assets.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.old_string:
def test_view_share_partial_grant_filters_aggregation(
    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
) -> None:
    """A view-share on ONLY s1 sees asset X but with suite_count=1 (s2 filtered
    out), and never sees asset Y — the partial-grant aggregation-filtering rule."""
    viewer = _user(client_db(client), "viewer@example.com")
    _share(client_db(client), world["s1"], viewer, "view")
    _as(viewer)
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/assets")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    by_id = {a["id"]: a for a in resp.json()}
    assert str(world["asset_x"]) in by_id
    # #920: asset Y (no grant) is present but REDACTED — never omitted, never named.
    y = by_id[str(world["asset_y"])]
    assert y["is_accessible"] is False and y["name"] is None and y["suite_count"] == 0
    assert by_id[str(world["asset_x"])]["suite_count"] == 1  # s2 filtered out
input.new_string:
def test_partial_grant_browse_is_workspace_true(
    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
) -> None:
    """ADR 0037: a view-share on ONLY s1 still sees asset X with suite_count=2 —
    the rollup aggregates over ALL composing suites, identical for every viewer

def test_404_no_leak_bodies_identical(client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """No-leak means indistinguishable: an existing-but-ungranted asset and a
    truly unknown id must return the same status AND the same body shape — the
    only permitted variation is the probed id echoed back in the detail."""
    outsider = _user(client_db(client), "outsider3@example.com")
    _as(outsider)
    unknown_id = uuid.uuid4()
    existing = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_x']}")
    unknown = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{unknown_id}")
    assert existing.status_code == unknown.status_code == 404

    def normalized(resp: Any) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
        body: dict[str, Any] = resp.json()
        echoed: str = body["error"]["detail"].pop("asset_id")
        return echoed, body

    existing_echo, existing_body = normalized(existing)
    unknown_echo, unknown_body = normalized(unknown)
    # The echoed id must be exactly the probed one (no other id leaks) …
    assert existing_echo == str(world["asset_x"])
    assert unknown_echo == str(unknown_id)
    # … and everything else must be byte-identical between the two cases.
    assert existing_body == unknown_body

def test_redacted_neighbour_identity_never_reaches_the_response_body(
    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
) -> None:
    """The claim #845 makes is that a restricted neighbour's identity **never crosses the
    wire** — so assert it at the wire, not at the DTO one layer above it.

    `LineageNodeRead` is a separate Pydantic model from the service dataclass. If someone
    later re-tightens `name: str` with an empty-string fallback, or a serializer starts
    deriving a display name, the leak would ship with a fully green suite unless something
    inspects the raw body. This does."""
    db = client_db(client)
    stranger = _user(db, "stranger2@example.com")
    conn = _connection(db, stranger)
    secret = _suite(db, stranger, conn, name="Secret mart", table="MART_SECRET_REVENUE")
    assert secret.asset_id is not None
    secret_asset = db.get(Asset, secret.asset_id)
    db.add(
        LineageEdge(
            upstream_asset_id=world["asset_x"],
            downstream_asset_id=secret.asset_id,
            source="dbt",
        )
    )
    db.commit()

    _as(world["owner"])
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_x']}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    node = next(n for n in resp.json()["downstream"] if n["id"] == str(secret.asset_id))
    assert node["is_accessible"] is False
    assert node["name"] is None and node["namespace"] is None and node["env"] is None
    assert node["is_monitored"] is False
    # The raw body — the actual wire. The name must survive nowhere in it.
    assert "MART_SECRET_REVENUE" not in resp.text
    assert secret_asset is not None and secret_asset.name not in resp.text

input.old_string:
def test_column_lineage_redacts_pairs_on_inaccessible_edges(
    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
) -> None:
    """#901 + #845 at the wire: an edge's column pairs are schema disclosure, so they
    follow the SAME one-rule as the node identity — a redacted endpoint collapses the
    mapping to a count-only box, and the column names never cross the wire. An
    accessible edge returns the full pairs (and the same count), so the redacted and
    open renderings are provably the same data minus the names."""
    db = client_db(client)
    stranger = _user(db, "stranger3@example.com")
    conn = _connection(db, stranger)
    secret = _suite(db, stranger, conn, name="Secret cols", table="MART_SECRET_COLS")
    assert secret.asset_id is not None
    db.add(
        LineageEdge(
            upstream_asset_id=world["asset_x"],
            downstream_asset_id=secret.asset_id,
            source="unity_catalog",
            connection_id=conn.id,
            columns=[["comment", "sentiment_secret_col"], ["customer_id", "customer_id"]],
        )
    )
    # A fully-visible edge with column data: asset_x -> asset_y (both the owner's).
    db.add(
        LineageEdge(
            upstream_asset_id=world["asset_x"],
            downstream_asset_id=world["asset_y"],
            source="unity_catalog",
            connection_id=conn.id,
            columns=[["order_id", "order_id"]],
        )
    )
    db.commit()

    _as(world["owner"])
    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/assets/{world['asset_x']}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    edges = {(e["source"], e["target"]): e for e in resp.json()["lineage_edges"]}

    redacted = edges[(str(world["asset_x"]), str(secret.asset_id))]
    assert redacted["columns"] is None
    assert redacted["column_count"] == 2
    # The wire itself: a hidden column name must survive nowhere in the body.
    assert "sentiment_secret_col" not in resp.text

    visible = edges[(str(world["asset_x"]), str(world["asset_y"]))]
    assert visible["columns"] == [["order_id", "order_id"]]
    assert visible["column_count"] == 1

    # A table-grain-only edge carries neither field populated.
    for e in resp.json()["lineage_edges"]:
        if (e["source"], e["target"]) not in (
            (str(world["asset_x"]), str(secret.asset_id)),
            (str(world["asset_x"]), str(world["asset_y"])),
        ):
            assert e["columns"] is None and e["column_count"] is None

    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any]
) -> None:
    """#901 under ADR 0037: column pairs are schema metadata — identity — so an
    edge to an asset monitored by someone else's suite still returns its pairs in
    full (the count-only redacted box is retired). Table-grain edges stay null."""
    db = client_db(client)
    stranger = _user(db, "stranger3@example.com")
    conn = _connection(db, stranger)

    db.add(

            columns=[["comment", "sentiment_col"], ["customer_id", "customer_id"]],
        )

    db.add(

            downstream_asset_id=world["asset_y"],

def test_browse_includes_out_of_grant_assets_as_redacted_rows(
    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any], make_workspace_admin: Any
) -> None:
    """#920 (user-directed): browse INCLUDES assets monitored solely by suites the
    caller can't see — as redacted rows (the tree-level #845 rule: omission asserts
    "this schema holds nothing else"). Pinned at the wire: only id + namespace +
    the PARENT path cross; the leaf name, env, and every health fact stay home."""
    db = client_db(client)
    stranger = _user(db, "stranger4@example.com")
    conn = _connection(db, stranger)
    secret = _suite(db, stranger, conn, name="Secret browse", table="MART_SECRET_BROWSE")
    assert secret.asset_id is not None
    secret_asset = db.get(Asset, secret.asset_id)
    assert secret_asset is not None
    db.commit()

    _as(world["owner"])
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/assets", params={"limit": 200})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    rows = {r["id"]: r for r in resp.json()}

    row = rows[str(secret.asset_id)]
    assert row["is_accessible"] is False
    assert row["name"] is None and row["env"] is None and row["description"] is None
    assert row["owner_user_id"] is None
    # Placement is the deliberate disclosure: namespace + the non-leaf path only.
    assert row["namespace"] == secret_asset.namespace
    leaf = secret_asset.name.rsplit(".", 1)[-1]
    assert row["name_prefix_segments"] == secret_asset.name.split(".")[:-1]
    assert row["last_seen"] is None  # liveness cadence is a fact about the asset
    # Health/monitoredness are facts about the hidden asset — all at empty defaults.
    assert row["suite_count"] == 0 and row["checks_total"] == 0
    assert row["worst_severity"] is None and row["has_failed_run"] is False
    # The wire itself: the leaf name must survive nowhere in the body.
    assert leaf not in resp.text

    # The owner's own assets are untouched full rows.
    mine = rows[str(world["asset_x"])]
    assert mine["is_accessible"] is True and mine["name"] is not None

    # A workspace admin still sees the full row (no redaction for include_all).
    admin = _user(db, _ADMIN_EMAIL)
    make_workspace_admin(_ADMIN_EMAIL)
    _as(admin)
    admin_rows = {r["id"]: r for r in client.get("/api/v1/assets", params={"limit": 200}).json()}
    assert admin_rows[str(secret.asset_id)]["name"] == secret_asset.name

    client: TestClient, world: dict[str, Any], make_workspace_admin: Any
) -> None:
    """ADR 0037 pinned at the wire: the browse row for an asset monitored solely by
    a stranger's suite is BYTE-IDENTICAL for the stranger, an unrelated member, and
    a workspace admin — identity and the workspace-true rollup, one truth for all."""
    db = client_db(client)
    stranger = _user(db, "stranger4@example.com")
    conn = _connection(db, stranger)

    def row_for(user: User) -> dict[str, Any]:
        _as(user)
        resp = client.get("/api/v1/assets", params={"limit": 200})

warehouse's OWN lineage views straight into `lineage_edges` with `source='snowflake'` /
   `'unity_catalog'`: Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` (all editions) → `ACCESS_HISTORY` /
   `GET_LINEAGE` (Enterprise); Unity Catalog `system.access.table_lineage`. First-hand, no
   dbt hop. Daily beat, **dark by default** (`WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED` — the views need a
   grant); the tier that answered and any degraded/failing state surface on the asset's
   lineage graph so a view-level-only or stale graph never reads as a confident complete
   one ([details](orchestration.md#lineage-from-the-warehouse-warehouselineageprovider-858)).
   **Column grain (#901):** where the warehouse offers it (UC
   `system.access.column_lineage` — live-verified), the pull refines each table edge with
   `upstream column → downstream column` pairs, shown on the asset page; a mapping whose
   far endpoint is outside your grants renders as a **count-only redacted box** (the #845
   one-rule — column names of a hidden asset are schema disclosure). Snowflake's column
   grain lives in `ACCESS_HISTORY` (Enterprise) and reports honestly unavailable on
   Standard.

| Datasource | Asset entity | ① Run-stamping | ② dbt manifest | ③ OL emission | ④ Catalog pull | ⑤ Warehouse-native |
|---|---|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
| Snowflake | `snowflake://{org}-{account}` / `DB.SCHEMA.TABLE` | ✅ | ✅ (live-verified) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ (OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES live; ACCESS_HISTORY/GET_LINEAGE Enterprise) |
| Unity Catalog | `unitycatalog://{host}` / `catalog.schema.table` | ✅ | ✅ (adapter-aware) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ (system.access.table_lineage, incremental; **+ column grain, live-verified**) |
| ADLS Gen2 (files) | `abfss://{container}@{account}.dfs.core.windows.net` / pattern **base prefix** | ✅ | — | ✅ | ✅ | — |
| S3 (files) | `s3://{bucket}` / base prefix | ✅ | — | ✅ | ✅ | — |

  routing engine; it's a recipient-resolution function.

## 4. Authz & surfaces

- **Asset visibility = derived, not granted.** An asset is visible iff the caller can
  `view` ≥1 suite mapped to it; the asset page aggregates **only** the
  suites/results the caller's grants cover (ADR 0027 ladder untouched, 404-no-leak
  for assets wholly outside one's grants). Same rule for incidents (visible via the
  incident's suite). Workspace-admins see all (0027); Viewers cap at `view` (0033).
- **Asset metadata mutations** (assign owner, description): **workspace-Admin-only**
  at first — the cheap, safe row on the 0033 matrix; widen to composing-suite `edit`
  later if it chafes.
- **UI phasing** (phase 1 — the asset entity — has no UI): (2) read-only Assets list + asset page (health across suites) →
